text.KJV: 7:4 And mine eye shal not spare thee, neither will I haue pitie: but I will recompense thy wayes vpon thee, and thine abominations shalbe in the midst of thee, and yee shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 7:4 My eye will not spare you, nor will I have pity; but I will hold your conduct against you, since your abominations remain within you; then shall you know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Disaster after disaster! See, it comes.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh:A calamity, a sole calamity, lo! it hath come.
text.KJV: 7:5 Thus sayth the Lord God, An euill, an onely euill, behold, is come.
text.NABRE: 7:5 Thus says the Lord God: Evil upon evil! See it coming!

text.NRSV: An end has come, the end has come. It has awakened against you; see, it comes!
text.Rotherham: An end hath come, Come hath the end It hath roused itself up against thee,— Lo! it hath come.
text.KJV: 7:6 An end is come, the end is come, it watcheth for thee, behold, it is come.
text.NABRE: 7:6 An end is coming, the end is coming; it is ripe for you! See it coming!

text.NRSV: Your doom has come to you, O inhabitant of the land. The time has come, the day is near—of tumult, not of reveling on the mountains.
text.Rotherham: The circle hath come round unto thee. O inhabitant of the land,- The time, hath come, The day of consternation, hath drawn near. And not the joyful shout of the mountains.
text.KJV: 7:7 The morning is come vnto thee, O thou that dwellest in the land: the time is come, the day of trouble is neere, and not the sounding againe of the mountaines.
text.NABRE: 7:7 The crisis has come for you who dwell in the land! The time has come, near is the day: panic, no rejoicing on the mountains.

text.NRSV: Soon I will pour out my fury upon you; I will spend my anger against you. I will judge you according to your ways, and punish you for all your abominations.
text.Rotherham: Now shortly, will I pour out mine indignation upon thee, And will bring to an end mine anger against thee, And will judge thee according to thy ways,- And will lay upon thee all thine abominations.
text.KJV: 7:8 Now will I shortly powre out my furie vpon thee, and accomplish mine anger vpon thee: and I wil iudge thee according to thy wayes, and will recompense thee for all thine abominations.
text.NABRE: 7:8 Soon now I will pour out my fury upon you and spend my anger against you; I will judge you according to your ways and hold against you all your abominations.

text.NRSV: My eye will not spare, I will have no pity. I will punish you according to your ways, while your abominations are among you. Then you shall know that it is I the Lord who strike.
text.Rotherham: And mine eye shall not shield thee Neither will I have pity,— According to thy ways, unto thee will I render, And thine abominations, in thy midst shall be found, So shall ye know that I, Yahweh am smiting.
text.KJV: 7:9 And mine eye shall not spare, neither will I haue pitie: I will recompense thee according to thy wayes, and thine abominations that are in the middest of thee, and yee shall know that I am the Lord that smiteth.
text.NABRE: 7:9 My eye will not spare, nor will I take pity; I will hold your conduct against you since your abominations remain within you, then you shall know that it is I, the Lord, who strikes.

text.NRSV: See, the day! See, it comes! Your doom has gone out. The rod has blossomed, pride has budded.
text.Rotherham: Lo! the day, Lo! it hath come, The circle, hath gone forth, The sceptre, hath blossomed, Insolence hath sprouted:
text.KJV: 7:10 Behold the day, behold, it is come, the morning is gone foorth, the rodde hath blossomed, pride hath budded.
text.NABRE: 7:10 The day is here! Look! it is coming! The crisis has come! Lawlessness is blooming, insolence budding;

text.NRSV: Violence has grown into a rod of wickedness. None of them shall remain, not their abundance, not their wealth; no pre-eminence among them.
text.Rotherham: Violence hath risen up as a sceptre of lawlessness, —There are none of them And none of their multitude And none of their throngs And no lamentation over them.
text.KJV: 7:11 Uiolence is risen vp into a rod of wickednesse: none of them shall remaine, nor of their multitude, nor of any of theirs, neither shall there be wailing for them.
text.NABRE: 7:11 the violent have risen up to wield a scepter of wickedness. But none of them shall remain; none of their crowd, none of their wealth, for none of them are innocent.

text.NRSV: The time has come, the day draws near; let not the buyer rejoice, nor the seller mourn, for wrath is upon all their multitude.
text.Rotherham: The time hath come. The day hath arrived, The buyer, let him not rejoice, and The seller, let him not mourn,— For indignation, is against all her multitude.
text.KJV: 7:12 The time is come, the day draweth neere, let not the buyer reioyce, nor the seller mourne: for wrath is vpon all the multitude thereof.
text.NABRE: 7:12 The time has come, the day dawns. The buyer must not rejoice, nor the seller mourn, for wrath is coming upon all the throng.

text.NRSV: For the sellers shall not return to what has been sold as long as they remain alive. For the vision concerns all their multitude; it shall not be revoked. Because of their iniquity, none can maintain their life.
text.Rotherham: For the seller, unto that which is to be sold, shall not return, though yet among the living, were their life, for the vision is against all her multitude. He shall not return, And, no man, by his punishment, shall strengthen, his life.
text.KJV: 7:13 For the seller shall not returne to that which is solde, although they were yet aliue: for the vision is touching the whole multitude thereof which shal not returne: neither shall any strengthen himselfe in the iniquity of his life.
text.NABRE: 7:13 Assuredly, the seller shall not regain what was sold, as long as they all live; for the vision is for the whole crowd: it shall not be revoked! Yes, because of their guilt, they shall not hold on to life.

text.NRSV: They have blown the horn and made everything ready; but no one goes to battle, for my wrath is upon all their multitude.
text.Rotherham: They have blown the trumpet, even to make All ready, Yet is there none going to the battle; For mine indignation is against all her multitude.
text.KJV: 7:14 They haue blowen the trumpet, euen to make all ready, but none goeth to the battell: for my wrath is vpon all the multitude thereof.
text.NABRE: 7:14 They will sound the trumpet and get everything ready, but no one will go out to battle, for my wrath weighs upon all the crowd.

text.NRSV: The sword is outside, pestilence and famine are inside; those in the open field shall die by the sword; and, him that is in the city, famine and pestilence shall devour.
text.Rotherham: The sword without, and Pestilence and famine within,— He that is in the field by the sword, shall die, and He that is in the city famine or pestilence shall devour him;
text.KJV: 7:15 The sword is without, and the pestilence and the famine within: he that is in the field shall die with the sword, and hee that is in the city, famine and pestilence shall deuoure him.
text.NABRE: 7:15 The sword is outside; disease and hunger are within. Whoever is in the fields will die by the sword; whoever is in the city disease and hunger will devour.

text.NRSV: If any survivors escape, they shall be on the mountains like doves of the valleys, all of them moaning over their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: While they who escape of them shall escape and become on the mountains as the doves of the valleys, all of them cooing,—each one in his punishment.
text.KJV: 7:16 ¶ But they that escape of them, shall escape, and shall be on the mountaines like doues of the valleys, all of them mourning, euery one for his iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 7:16 If their survivors flee, they will die on the mountains, moaning like doves of the valley on account of their guilt.

text.NRSV: All hands shall grow feeble, all knees turn to water.
text.Rotherham: All hands shall be unnerved; and All knees shall be weak as water,
text.KJV: 7:17 All hands shall be feeble, and all knees shalbe weake as water.
text.NABRE: 7:17 All their hands will hang limp, and all their knees turn to water.

text.NRSV: They shall gird themselves with sackcloth, and horror shall cover them; shame shall be on all faces, and baldness on all their heads.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they gird themselves with sackcloth, And shuddering shall cover them,-And in all faces, shall be paleness, And in all their heads, baldness.
text.KJV: 7:18 They shall also girde themselues with sackcloth, and horrour shall couer them, and shame shall be vpon all faces, and baldnesse vpon all their heads.
text.NABRE: 7:18 They put on sackcloth, horror clothes them; shame is on all their faces, all their heads are shaved bald.

text.NRSV: They shall flung their silver into the streets, and their gold shall be like an unclean thing. Their silver and gold cannot save them on the day of the wrath of the Lord. They shall not satisfy their hunger or fill their stomachs with it. For it was the stumbling block of their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Their silver into the streets, shall they cast and Their gold for throwing away, shall serve, Their silver and their gold shall not be able to deliver them in the day of the wrath of Yahweh, Their craving, shall they not satisfy, and Their belly, shall they not fill, For a stumbling-block, hath their iniquity become.
text.KJV: 7:19 They shall cast their siluer in the streets, and their golde shalbe remooued: their siluer and their golde shall not be able to deliuer them in the day of the wrath of the Lord: they shall not satisfie their soules, neither fill their bowels: because it is the stumbling blocke of their iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 7:19 They fling their silver into the streets, and their gold is considered unclean. Their silver and gold cannot save them on the day of the Lord ’s wrath. They cannot satisfy their hunger or fill their bellies, for it has been the occasion of their sin.

text.NRSV: From their beautiful ornament, in which they took pride, they made their abominable images, their detestable things; therefore I will make of it an unclean thing to them.
text.Rotherham: When, the beauty of his own ornament, he had, in majesty, placed, Then, the images, of their abominations Their detestable things, Made they therein,— For this cause have I delivered it up to them for removal;
text.KJV: 7:20 ¶ As for the beautie of his ornament, he set it in maiestie: but they made the images of their abominations, and of their detestable things therein: therefore haue I set it farre from them.
text.NABRE: 7:20 In their beautiful ornaments they took pride; out of them they made their abominable images, their detestable things. For this reason I will make them unclean.

text.NRSV: I will hand it over to strangers as booty, to the wicked of the earth as plunder; they shall profane it.
text.Rotherham: Yea I will deliver it Into the hand of foreigners for a prey, And to the lawless ones of the earth for a spoil, And they will profane it;
text.KJV: 7:21 And I will giue it into the hands of the strangers for a pray, and to the wicked of the earth for a spoile, and they shall pollute it.
text.NABRE: 7:21 I will hand them over as spoils to foreigners, as plunder to the wicked of the earth, so that they may defile them.

text.NRSV: I will avert my face from them, so that they may profane my treasured place; robbers shall enter it and profane it.
text.Rotherham: And I will turn away my face from them, And they will profane my cherished place,—Yea there will enter it violent men Who will profane it.
text.KJV: 7:22 My face will I turne also from them, and they shall pollute my secret place: for the robbers shall enter into it and defile it.
text.NABRE: 7:22 I will turn my face away from them. My treasure will be defiled; the violent will enter and defile it.

text.NRSV: Make a chain! For the land is full of bloody crimes, and the city is full of violence.
text.Rotherham: Prepare thou a chain,— For, the land, is full of the crime of bloodshed, And, the city, is full of violence.
text.KJV: 7:23 ¶ Make a chaine: for the land is full of bloody crimes, the citie is full of violence.
text.NABRE: 7:23 They will wreak slaughter, for the land is filled with bloodshed and the city with violence.

text.NRSV: I will bring the worst of the nations to take possession of their houses. I will put an end to the arrogance of the strong, and their holy places shall be profaned.
text.Rotherham: So then I will bring in the wicked ones of the nations, And they shall take possession of their houses,—And I will cause to cease the pomp of the strong ones, And their holy places shall be profaned.
text.KJV: 7:24 Wherfore I will bring the worst of the heathen, and they shall possesse their houses: I will also make the pompe of the strong to cease, and their holy places shall be defiled.
text.NABRE: 7:24 I will bring in the worst of the nations to take possession of their houses. I will put an end to their proud strength, and their sanctuaries will be defiled.

text.NRSV: When anguish comes, they shall seek peace, but there shall be none.
text.Rotherham: Destruction, hath entered,- And they shall seek welfare and there be none.
text.KJV: 7:25 Destruction commeth, and they shall seeke peace, and there shall be none.
text.NABRE: 7:25 When anguish comes, they will seek peace, but there is none.

text.NRSV: Disaster comes upon disaster, rumor follows rumor; they shall keep seeking a vision from the prophet; instruction shall perish from the priest, and counsel from the elders.
text.Rotherham: Disaster upon disaster, shall come, And report after report shall arise, Therefore shall they seek a vision from prophet, And law shall perish from priest. And counsel from elders.
text.KJV: 7:26 Mischiefe shall come vpon mischiefe, and rumour shall be vpon rumour, then shall they seeke a vision of the prophet: but the law shall perish from the priest, and counsell from the ancients.
text.NABRE: 7:26 Disaster after disaster, rumor upon rumor. They keep seeking a vision from the prophet; instruction from the priest is missing, and counsel from the elders.

text.NRSV: The king shall mourn, the prince shall be clothed with desolation, and the hands of the people of the land shall tremble. According to their way I will deal with them; according to their own judgments I will judge them. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: The king, shall mourn and, The chief, shall clothe himself with astonishment, and, the hands of the people of the land, shall be wrung in anguish,— After their own way, will I deal with them and, With their own sentences, will I sentence them, And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 7:27 The king shall mourne, and the prince shall be clothed with desolation, and the hands of the people of the land shall be troubled: I will doe vnto them after their way, and according to their deserts will I iudge them, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 7:27 The king mourns, the prince is terror-stricken, the hands of the common people tremble. I will deal with them according to their ways, and according to their judgments I will judge them. They shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: In the sixth year, in the sixth month, on the fifth day of the month, as I sat in my house, with the elders of Judah sitting before me, the hand of the Lord God fell upon me there.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the sixth year in the sixth month, on the fifth of the month, I being seated in my house, and the elders of Judah being seated before me, then fell upon me there the hand of My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 8:1 And it came to passe in the sixt yeere, in the sixt moneth, in the fift day of the month, as I sate in mine house, and the elders of Iudah sate before me; that the hand of the Lord God fell there vpon me.
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 In the sixth year, on the fifth day of the sixth month, as I was sitting in my house, with the elders of Judah sitting before me, the hand of the Lord God fell upon me there.

text.NRSV: I looked, and there was a figure that looked like a human being; below what appeared to be its loins it was fire, and above the loins it was like the appearance of brightness, like gleaming amber.
text.Rotherham: So I looked, and lo! a likeness as the appearance of a man, from the appearance of his loins and downward fire, and from his loins and upwards as an appearance of shining, as the look of amber,
text.KJV: 8:2 Then I beheld, and loe, a likenesse as the appearance of fire: from the appearance of his loines euen downeward, fire: and from his loines euen vpward, as the appearance of brightnesse, as the colour of amber.
text.NABRE: 8:2 I looked up and there was a figure that looked like a man. Downward from what looked like his waist, there was fire; from his waist upward, like the brilliance of polished bronze.

text.NRSV: It stretched out the form of a hand, and took me by a lock of my head; and the spirit lifted me up between earth and heaven, and brought me in visions of God to Jerusalem, to the entrance of the gateway of the inner court that faces north, where the seat of the image of jealousy, which provokes to jealousy, was located.
text.Rotherham: Then put he forth the similitude of a hand, and took me by the forelock of my head,—and the Spirit lifted me up between the earth and the heavens and brought me to Jerusalem in the visions of God into the opening of the inner gate that looketh toward the north, where was the seat of the Statue of Jealousy. that provoketh to jealousy;
text.KJV: 8:3 And he put forth the forme of an hand, and tooke me by a locke of mine head, and the spirit lift me vp betweene the earth and the heauen, and brought me in the visions of God to Ierusalem, to the doore of the inner gate, that looketh toward the North, where was the seate of the image of ielousie, which prouoketh to ielousie.
text.NABRE: 8:3 Vision of Abominations in the Temple. He stretched out the form of a hand and seized me by the hair of my head. The spirit lifted me up between earth and heaven and brought me in divine vision to Jerusalem to the entrance of the inner gate facing north where the statue of jealousy that provokes jealousy stood.

text.NRSV: And the glory of the God of Israel was there, like the vision that I had seen in the valley.
text.Rotherham: and lo! there, the glory of the God of Israel, like the appearance which I had seen in the valley.
text.KJV: 8:4 And behold, the glory of the God of Israel was there according to the vision that I saw in the plaine.
text.NABRE: 8:4 There I saw the glory of the God of Israel, like the vision I had seen in the plain.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “O mortal, lift up your eyes now toward the north.” So I lifted up my eyes toward the north, and there, north of the altar gate, in the entrance, was this image of jealousy.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man Lift up. I pray thee thine eyes, the way toward the north. So I lifted up mine eyes the way toward the north, and lo! on the north by the gate of the altar, this Statue of Jealousy, in the entrance.
text.KJV: 8:5 ¶ Then said he vnto me, Sonne of man, lift vp thine eyes now the way towards the North: so I lift vp mine eyes the way toward the North, and behold, Northward at the gate of the altar, this image of ielousie in the entry.
text.NABRE: 8:5 He said to me: Son of man, lift your eyes to the north! I looked to the north and there in the entry north of the altar gate was this statue of jealousy.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Mortal, do you see what they are doing, the great abominations that the house of Israel are committing here, to drive me far from my sanctuary? Yet you will see still greater abominations.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man Canst thou see what they are doing,—the great abominations which the house of Israel are committing here, that I should go faraway from my sanctuary? Howbeit yet again, shalt thou see great abominations.
text.KJV: 8:6 He said furthermore vnto me, Sonne of man, seest thou what they doe? euen the great abominations that the house of Israel committeth heere, that I should goe farre off from my sanctuarie? but turne thee yet againe, and thou shalt see greater abominations.
text.NABRE: 8:6 He asked, Son of man, do you see what they are doing? Do you see the great abominations that the house of Israel is practicing here, so that I must depart from my sanctuary? You shall see even greater abominations!

text.NRSV: And he brought me to the entrance of the court; I looked, and there was a hole in the wall.
text.Rotherham: So he brought me into the opening of the court,—and I looked, and lo! a single hole in the wall.
text.KJV: 8:7 ¶ And hee brought me to the doore of the court, and when I looked, behold a hole in the wall.
text.NABRE: 8:7 Then he brought me to the entrance of the courtyard, and there I saw a hole in the wall.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Mortal, dig through the wall”; and when I digged through the wall, there was a door.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me Son of man Break I pray thee through the wall. So I broke through the wall, and lo! a single opening.
text.KJV: 8:8 Then said he vnto me, Sonne of man, digge now in the wall: and when I had digged in the wall, behold a doore.
text.NABRE: 8:8 Son of man, he ordered, dig through the wall. I dug through the wall—there was a doorway.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Go in, and see the vile abominations that they are committing here.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me,— Go in, and see the wicked abominations. which they are committing here.
text.KJV: 8:9 And he said vnto me, Goe in, and behold the wicked abominations that they doe heere.
text.NABRE: 8:9 Go in, he said to me, and see the evil abominations they are doing here.

text.NRSV: So I went in and looked; there, portrayed on the wall all around, were all kinds of creeping things, and loathsome animals, and all the idols of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: So I went in. and looked, and lo! every similitude of creeping thing, and detestable beast, and all the manufactured gods of the house of Israel,- portrayed upon the wall round about on every side;
text.KJV: 8:10 So I went in and saw, and behold euery forme of creeping things, and abominable beasts, and all the idols of the house of Israel purtrayed vpon the wall round about.
text.NABRE: 8:10 I went in and looked—figures of all kinds of creeping things and loathsome beasts, all the idols of the house of Israel, pictured around the wall.

text.NRSV: Before them stood seventy of the elders of the house of Israel, with Jaazaniah son of Shaphan standing among them. Each had his censer in his hand, and the fragrant cloud of incense was ascending.
text.Rotherham: and seventy men of the elders of the house of Israel with Jaazaniah son of Shaphan, who stood in their midst were standing before them, even every man with his censer in his hand,—and the fragrance of the cloud of incense ascending.
text.KJV: 8:11 And there stood before them seuentie men of the ancients of the house of Israel, and in the middest of them stood Iaazaniah the sonne of Shaphan, with euery man his censer in his hand, and a thicke cloud of incense went vp.
text.NABRE: 8:11 Before them stood seventy of the elders of the house of Israel. Among them stood Jaazaniah, son of Shaphan, each with censer in hand; a cloud of incense drifted upward.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Mortal, have you seen what the elders of the house of Israel are doing in the dark, each in his room of images? For they say, ‘The Lord does not see us, the Lord has forsaken the land.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Hast thou seen Son of man what the elders of the house of Israel, are doing in the dark, every man in his image-chambers? for they are saying— Yahweh doth not see us! Yahweh hath forsaken the land!
text.KJV: 8:12 Then said he vnto me, Sonne of man, hast thou seene what the ancients of the house of Israel doe in the darke, euery man in the chambers of his imagery? for they say, The Lord seeth vs not, the Lord hath forsaken the earth.
text.NABRE: 8:12 Then he said to me: Do you see, son of man, what the elders of the house of Israel are doing in the dark, each in his idol chamber? They think: “The Lord cannot see us; the Lord has forsaken the land.”

text.NRSV: He said also to me, “You will see still greater abominations that they are committing.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me,— Yet again, shalt thou see great abominations, which they are committing.
text.KJV: 8:13 ¶ Hee said also vnto me, Turne thee yet againe, and thou shalt see greater abominations that they doe.
text.NABRE: 8:13 He said: You will see them practicing even greater abominations.

text.NRSV: Then he brought me to the entrance of the north gate of the house of the Lord; women were sitting there weeping for Tammuz.
text.Rotherham: So he brought me into the opening of the gate of the house of Yahweh, which was toward the north,—and Lo! there, women sitting, weeping for Tammuz.
text.KJV: 8:14 Then he brought me to the doore of the gate of the Lords house which was towards the North, and behold, there sate women weeping for Tammuz.
text.NABRE: 8:14 Then he brought me to the entrance of the north gate of the house of the Lord. There women sat and wept for Tammuz.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Have you seen this, O mortal? You will see still greater abominations than these.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me Hast thou seen O son of man? Yet again, shalt thou see greater abominations than these.
text.KJV: 8:15 ¶ Then said hee vnto me, Hast thou seene this, O sonne of man? Turne thee yet againe, and thou shalt see greater abominations then these.
text.NABRE: 8:15 He said to me: Do you see this, son of man? You will see other abominations, greater than these!

text.NRSV: And he brought me into the inner court of the house of the Lord; there, at the entrance of the temple of the Lord, between the porch and the altar, were about twenty-five men, with their backs to the temple of the Lord, and their faces toward the east, prostrating themselves to the sun toward the east.
text.Rotherham: So he brought me into the inner court of the house of Yahweh, and lo! at the opening of the temple of Yahweh, between the porch and the altar, about twenty-five men,—their backs towards the temple of Yahweh and their faces eastward, and they were bowing down eastward unto the Sun.
text.KJV: 8:16 And he brought me into the inner court of the Lords house, and behold at the doore of the Temple of the Lord, betweene the porch and the altar, were about fiue and twentie men, with their backes toward the temple of the Lord, and their faces towards the East, and they worshipped the sunne towards the East.
text.NABRE: 8:16 Then he brought me into the inner court of the house of the Lord. There at the door of the Lord ’s temple, between the porch and the altar, were about twenty-five men with their backs to the Lord ’s temple and their faces toward the east; they were bowing eastward to the sun.

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “Have you seen this, O mortal? Is it too slight a thing for the house of Judah to commit the abominations that they are committing here, that they must fill the land with violence, and provoke me to anger over and over again? See, they are putting the branch to their nose!
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me Hast thou seen O son of man? Is it too small a thing for the house of Judah, to be committing the abominations which they have committed here,—that they have filled the land with violence and have again provoked me to anger and there they are! putting the branch to my nose.
text.KJV: 8:17 ¶ Then he said vnto me, Hast thou seene this, O sonne of man? Is it a light thing to the house of Iudah, that they commit the abominations, which they commit heere? for they haue filled the land with violence, and haue returned to prouoke me to anger: and loe, they put the branch to their nose.
text.NABRE: 8:17 He said: Do you see, son of man? Are the abominable things the house of Judah has done here so slight that they should also fill the land with violence, provoking me again and again? Now they are putting the branch to my nose!

text.NRSV: Therefore I will act in fury; my eye will not spare, nor will I have pity; and though they cry in my hearing with a loud voice, I will not listen to them.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore even I, will act with indignation, Mine eye shall not shield. Neither will I pity,— Though they have cried in mine ears with a loud voice, yet will I not hear them.
text.KJV: 8:18 Therefore will I also deale in furie: mine eye shall not spare, neither will I haue pitie: and though they crie in mine eares with a loud voyce, yet will I not heare them.
text.NABRE: 8:18 Therefore I in turn will act furiously: my eye will not spare, nor will I take pity. Even if they cry out in a loud voice for me to hear, I shall not listen to them.

text.NRSV: Then he cried in my hearing with a loud voice, saying, “Draw near, you executioners of the city, each with his destroying weapon in his hand.”
text.Rotherham: Then cried he in mine ears with a loud voice, saying, Draw near ye that have charge of the city,-even every man with his weapon of destruction in his hand.
text.KJV: 9:1 Hee cryed also in mine eares, with a loude voyce, saying; Cause them that hauecharge ouer the citie, to draw neere, euen euery man with his destroying weapon in his hand.
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 Slaughter of the Idolaters. Then he cried aloud for me to hear: Come, you scourges of the city!

text.NRSV: And six men came from the direction of the upper gate, which faces north, each with his weapon for slaughter in his hand; among them was a man clothed in linen, with a writing case at his side. They went in and stood beside the bronze altar.
text.Rotherham: And lo! six men coming in out of the way of the upper gate which looketh toward the north even every man with his destructive weapon in his hand, and one man in their midst clothed with linen, having a scribe’s ink holder by his side,—so they came in and stood beside the altar of bronze.
text.KJV: 9:2 And behold, sixe men came from the way of the higher gate, which lyeth toward the North, and euery man a slaughter weapon in his hand: and one man among them was clothed with linnen, with a writers inkehorne by his side, and they went in and stood beside the brasen altar.
text.NABRE: 9:2 And there were six men coming from the direction of the upper gate which faces north, each with a weapon of destruction in his hand. In their midst was a man dressed in linen, with a scribe’s case at his waist. They entered and stood beside the bronze altar.

text.NRSV: Now the glory of the God of Israel had gone up from the cherub on which it rested to the threshold of the house. The Lord called to the man clothed in linen, who had the writing case at his side,
text.Rotherham: And the glory of the God of Israel lifted itself up from off the cherub whereon it had been, unto the threshold of the house,—and he called unto the man clothed with linen, who had the scribe’s ink holder by his side.
text.KJV: 9:3 And the glory of the God of Israel was gone vp from the Cherub whereupon hee was, to the threshold of the house, and he called to the man clothed with linnen, which had the writers inkehorne by his side.
text.NABRE: 9:3 Then the glory of the God of Israel moved off the cherub and went up to the threshold of the temple. He called to the man dressed in linen with the scribe’s case at his waist,

text.NRSV: and said to him, “Go through the city, through Jerusalem, and put a mark on the foreheads of those who sigh and groan over all the abominations that are committed in it.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto him. Pass along through the midst of the city, through the midst of Jerusalem,—and set thou a mark upon the foreheads of the men who are sighing and crying over all the abominations that are being done in her midst.
text.KJV: 9:4 And the Lord sayd vnto him, Goe through the middest of the citie, through the middest of Ierusalem, and set a marke vpon the foreheads of the men that sigh, and that cry for all the abominations, that bee done in the middest thereof.
text.NABRE: 9:4 and the Lord said to him: Pass through the city, through the midst of Jerusalem, and mark an X on the foreheads of those who grieve and lament over all the abominations practiced within it.

text.NRSV: To the others he said in my hearing, “Pass through the city after him, and kill; your eye shall not spare, and you shall have no pity.
text.Rotherham: But unto these, said he in mine ears, Pass along through the city after him and smite,—let not your eye, shield, neither have ye pity:
text.KJV: 9:5 ¶ And to the others he said in mine hearing, Goe ye after him through the citie, and smite: let not your eye spare, neither haue ye pitie.
text.NABRE: 9:5 To the others he said in my hearing: Pass through the city after him and strike! Do not let your eyes spare; do not take pity.

text.NRSV: Cut down old men, young men and young women, little children and women; but touch no one who has the mark. And begin at my sanctuary.” So they began with the elders who were in front of the house.
text.Rotherham: elder, young man and virgin and little ones and women, shall ye slay utterly, but unto any man who hath upon him the mark, do not ye come near, and at my sanctuary, shall ye begin. So they began with the elder men. who were before the house.
text.KJV: 9:6 Slay vtterly olde and yong; both maides, and litle children, and women: but come not neere any man vpon whom is the marke, and begin at my sanctuary: then they began at the ancient men which were before the house.
text.NABRE: 9:6 Old and young, male and female, women and children—wipe them out! But do not touch anyone marked with the X. Begin at my sanctuary. So they began with the elders who were in front of the temple.

text.NRSV: Then he said to them, “Defile the house, and fill the courts with the slain. Go!” So they went out and killed in the city.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them Defile ye the house and fill the courts with the slain,—go ye forth! So they went forth and smote in the city.
text.KJV: 9:7 And hee sayd vnto them, Defile the house, and fill the courts with the slaine, goe ye forth: and they went forth and slew in the citie.
text.NABRE: 9:7 Defile the temple, he said to them, fill its courts with the slain. Then go out and strike in the city.

text.NRSV: While they were killing, and I was left alone, I fell on my face and cried out, “Ah, Lord God! will you destroy all who remain of Israel as you pour out your fury upon Jerusalem?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, while they were smiting them—I being left remaining, that I fell upon my face and made outcry, and said Alas! My Lord. Yahweh art thou about to destroy all the remnant of Israel, in that thou art pouring out of thine indignation upon Jerusalem?
text.KJV: 9:8 ¶ And it came to passe while they were slaying them, and I was left, that I fell vpon my face, and cryed and said, Ah, Lord God, wilt thou destroy all the residue of Israel, in thy powring out of thy fury vpon Ierusalem?
text.NABRE: 9:8 As they were striking, I was left alone. I fell on my face, crying out, “Alas, Lord God! Will you destroy all that is left of Israel when you pour out your fury on Jerusalem?”

text.NRSV: He said to me, “The guilt of the house of Israel and Judah is exceedingly great; the land is full of bloodshed and the city full of perversity; for they say, ‘The Lord has forsaken the land, and the Lord does not see.’
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me the iniquity of the house of Israel and Judah, is exceeding, great, and the land is filled with shed blood, and the city is full of over-reaching,—for they have said. Yahweh hath forsaken the land,— and Yahweh doth not see.
text.KJV: 9:9 Then sayd he vnto me; The iniquity of the house of Israel and Iudah is exceeding great, and the land is full of blood, and the citie full of peruersenesse: for they say; The Lord hath forsaken the earth, and the Lord seeth not.
text.NABRE: 9:9 He answered me: The guilt of the house of Israel and the house of Judah is too great to measure; the land is filled with bloodshed, the city with lawlessness. They think that the Lord has abandoned the land, that he does not see them.

text.NRSV: As for me, my eye will not spare, nor will I have pity, but I will bring their deeds upon their heads.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore as for even me, Mine eye shall not shield. Neither will I pity,— Their way upon their own head, have I rendered.
text.KJV: 9:10 And as for me also, mine eye shal not spare, neither will I haue pitie, but I will recompence their way vpon their head.
text.NABRE: 9:10 My eye, however, will not spare, nor shall I take pity, but I will bring their conduct down upon their heads.

text.NRSV: Then the man clothed in linen, with the writing case at his side, brought back word, saying, “I have done as you commanded me.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! the man clothed in linen, having the ink-holder by his side, bringing back word saying,— I have done according to all which thou didst command me.
text.KJV: 9:11 And behold, the man clothed with linnen, which had the inkehorne by his side, reported the matter, saying; I haue done as thou hast commanded me.
text.NABRE: 9:11 Just then the man dressed in linen with the scribe’s case at his waist made his report: “I have done as you commanded!”

text.NRSV: Then I looked, and on the dome over the heads of the cherubim there appeared above them something like a sapphire, in form resembling a throne.
text.Rotherham: Then looked I and lo! in the expanse which was over the head of the cherubim as a sapphire stone, as the appearance of the likeness of a throne, appeared over them.
text.KJV: 10:1 Then I looked, and beholde, in the firmament that was aboue the head of the Cherubims, there appeared ouer them as it were a Saphir stone, as the appearance of the likenesse of a throne.
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 Then I looked and there above the firmament over the heads of the cherubim was something like a sapphire, something that looked like a throne.

text.NRSV: He said to the man clothed in linen, “Go within the wheelwork underneath the cherubim; fill your hands with burning coals from among the cherubim, and scatter them over the city.” He went in as I looked on.
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto the man clothed in linen—then said he Go in between the whirling wheels, even under the cherub, and fill both thy hands with live coals of fire from between the cherubim, and throw over the city. So he went in before mine eyes.
text.KJV: 10:2 And hee spake vnto the man clothed with linnen, and sayd, Goe in between the wheeles, euen vnder the Cherub, and fill thine hand with coales of fire from betweene the Cherubims, and scatter them ouer the city. And he went in my sight.
text.NABRE: 10:2 And he said to the man dressed in linen: Go within the wheelwork under the cherubim; fill both your hands with burning coals from the place among the cherubim, then scatter them over the city. As I watched, he entered.

text.NRSV: Now the cherubim were standing on the south side of the house when the man went in; and a cloud filled the inner court.
text.Rotherham: Now the cherubim were standing on the right side of the house when the man went in,—and, the, cloud filled the inner court,
text.KJV: 10:3 Now the Cherubims stood on the right side of the house, when the man went in, and the cloud filled the inner court.
text.NABRE: 10:3 Now the cherubim were standing to the south of the temple when the man went in and a cloud filled the inner court.

text.NRSV: Then the glory of the Lord rose up from the cherub to the threshold of the house; the house was filled with the cloud, and the court was full of the brightness of the glory of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then arose the glory of Yahweh from off the cherub, unto the threshold of the house,—and the house was filled with the cloud, and the, court, was filled with the brightness of the glory of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 10:4 Then the glory of the Lord went vp from the Cherub, and stood ouer the threshold of the house, and the house was filled with the cloud, and the court was full of the brightnesse of the Lords glory.
text.NABRE: 10:4 The glory of the Lord had moved off the cherubim to the threshold of the temple; the temple was filled with the cloud, the whole court brilliant with the glory of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard as far as the outer court, like the voice of God Almighty when he speaks.
text.Rotherham: And the sound of the wings of the cherubim was heard, as far as the outer court,—like the voice of the Almighty GOD when he speaketh,
text.KJV: 10:5 And the sound of the Cherubims wings was heard euen to the vtter court, as the voice of the Almighty God when he speaketh.
text.NABRE: 10:5 The sound of the wings of the cherubim could be heard as far as the outer court; it was like the voice of God Almighty speaking.

text.NRSV: When he commanded the man clothed in linen, “Take fire from within the wheelwork, from among the cherubim,” he went in and stood beside a wheel.
text.Rotherham: So then it came to pass— when he commanded the man clothed with linen, saying, Take fire from between the whirling wheels, from between the cherubim, then went he in and stood beside the wheel.
text.KJV: 10:6 And it came to passe that when he had commanded the man clothed with linnen, saying; Take fire from betweene the wheeles, from betweene the Cherubims; then he went in and stood beside the wheeles.
text.NABRE: 10:6 He commanded the man dressed in linen: Take fire from within the wheelwork among the cherubim. The man entered and stood by one of the wheels.

text.NRSV: And a cherub stretched out his hand from among the cherubim to the fire that was among the cherubim, took some of it and put it into the hands of the man clothed in linen, who took it and went out.
text.Rotherham: And the cherub put forth his hand from between the cherubim unto the fire that was between the cherubim, and took up thereof and gave into the hands of him who was clothed with linen,—who took [it] and went forth,
text.KJV: 10:7 And one Cherub stretched forth his hand from betweene the Cherubims vnto the fire that was betweene the Cherubims: and tooke thereof, and put it into the handes of him that was clothed with linnen, who tooke it, and went out.
text.NABRE: 10:7 Thereupon a cherub stretched out a hand from among the cherubim toward the fire in the midst of the cherubim, took some, and put it in the hands of the one dressed in linen. He took it and came out.

text.NRSV: The cherubim appeared to have the form of a human hand under their wings.
text.Rotherham: Now the cherubim appeared to have. the likeness of the hand of a man, under their wings.
text.KJV: 10:8 ¶ And there appeared in the Cherubims, the forme of a mans hand vnder their wings.
text.NABRE: 10:8 Something like a human hand was visible under the wings of the cherubim.

text.NRSV: I looked, and there were four wheels beside the cherubim, one beside each cherub; and the appearance of the wheels was like gleaming beryl.
text.Rotherham: Then looked I. and lo! four wheels by the cherubim—one wheel by one cherub, and another wheel by another cherub,—and the appearance of the wheels was to look upon like a Tarshish stone;
text.KJV: 10:9 And when I looked, behold the foure wheeles by the Cherubims, one wheele by one Cherub, and an other wheele by an other Cherub: and the appearance of the wheeles was as the colour of a Berill stone.
text.NABRE: 10:9 I also saw four wheels beside the cherubim, one wheel beside each cherub, and the wheels appeared to have the sparkle of yellow topaz.

text.NRSV: And as for their appearance, the four had the same form, as though a wheel were within a wheel.
text.Rotherham: and as for their appearance, one likeness, had they four,-as might be a wheel in the midst of a wheel.
text.KJV: 10:10 And as for their appearances, they foure had one likenes as if a wheele had bene in the midst of a wheele.
text.NABRE: 10:10 And the appearance of the four all seemed alike, as though one wheel were inside the other.

text.NRSV: When they moved, they moved in any of the four directions without veering as they moved; but in whatever direction the front wheel faced, the others followed without veering as they moved.
text.Rotherham: When they went, upon their four sides, they went, they turned not when they went, for to the place whithersoever the head turned, after it, they went, they turned not when they went.
text.KJV: 10:11 When they went, they went vpon their foure sides; they turned not as they went, but to the place whither the head looked, they followed it; they turned not as they went.
text.NABRE: 10:11 When they moved, they went in any of the four directions without veering as they moved; in whatever direction the first cherub faced, the others followed without veering as they went.

text.NRSV: Their entire body, their backs, their hands, and their wings, as well as the wheels (the wheels of the four of them), were full of eyes all around.
text.Rotherham: And all their flesh, and their back, and their hands and their wings, and the wheels were full of eyes round about, even their wheels which they four had
text.KJV: 10:12 And their whole body, and their backes, and their handes, and their wings, and the wheeles, were ful of eyes round about, euen the wheeles that they foure had.
text.NABRE: 10:12 Their entire bodies—backs, hands, and wings—and wheels were covered with eyes all around like the four wheels.

text.NRSV: As for the wheels, they were called in my hearing “the wheelwork.”
text.Rotherham: To the wheels—to them, was made the cry— O whirling wheel! in mine ears.
text.KJV: 10:13 As for the wheeles, it was cried vnto them in my hearing, O wheele.
text.NABRE: 10:13 I heard the wheels called “wheelwork.”

text.NRSV: Each one had four faces: the first face was the face of the cherub, the second face was a human face, the third the face of a lion, and the fourth the face of an eagle.
text.Rotherham: And four faces had each one,—the first face, was the face of a cherub, and, the second face, was the face of a man and the third was the face of a lion. and the fourth, was the face of an eagle.
text.KJV: 10:14 And euery one had foure faces: the first face was the face of a Cherub, and the second face was the face of a man, and the third the face of a lion, and the fourth the face of an eagle.
text.NABRE: 10:14 Each living creature had four faces: the first a cherub, the second a human being, the third a lion, the fourth an eagle.

text.NRSV: Then the cherubim rose up. These were the living creatures that I had seen by the river Chebar.
text.Rotherham: Then arose the cherubim,—the same, was the living one, which I had seen by the river Chebar.
text.KJV: 10:15 And the Cherubims were lifted vp, this is the liuing creature that I saw by the riuer of Chebar.
text.NABRE: 10:15 When the cherubim rose up, they were indeed the living creatures I had seen by the river Chebar.

text.NRSV: When the cherubim moved, the wheels moved beside them; and when the cherubim lifted up their wings to rise up from the earth, the wheels at their side did not veer.
text.Rotherham: When the cherubim went, then went the wheels beside them,—and when the cherubim lifted up their wings, to arise from off the earth, the wheels themselves turned not from beside them;
text.KJV: 10:16 And when the Cherubims went, the wheeles went by them: and when the Cherubims lift vp their wings, to mount vp from the earth, the same wheels also turned not frō beside them.
text.NABRE: 10:16 When the cherubim moved, the wheels went beside them; when the cherubim lifted up their wings to rise from the earth, even then the wheels did not leave their sides.

text.NRSV: When they stopped, the others stopped, and when they rose up, the others rose up with them; for the spirit of the living creatures was in them.
text.Rotherham: when they stood, these stood, and when they arose, these arose with them,—for, the spirit of the living one, was in them.
text.KJV: 10:17 When they stood, these stood, and when they were lifted vp, these lift vp themselues also: for the spirit of the liuing creature was in them.
text.NABRE: 10:17 When they stood still, the wheels stood still; when they rose up, the wheels rose up with them, for the spirit of the living creatures was in them.

text.NRSV: Then the glory of the Lord went out from the threshold of the house and stopped above the cherubim.
text.Rotherham: And when the glory of Yahweh went forth from off the threshold of the house,—and stood over the cherubim,
text.KJV: 10:18 Then the glory of the Lord departed from off the threshold of the house, and stood ouer the Cherubims.
text.NABRE: 10:18 Then the glory of the Lord left the threshold of the temple and took its place upon the cherubim.

text.NRSV: The cherubim lifted up their wings and rose up from the earth in my sight as they went out, with the wheels beside them. They stopped at the entrance of the east gate of the house of the Lord; and the glory of the God of Israel was above them.
text.Rotherham: then the cherubim uplifted their wings and arose from the earth before mine eyes as they went forth, the wheels also in unison with them,—and it stood at the opening of the gate of the house of Yahweh that was toward the east, with the glory of the God of Israel over them, above.
text.KJV: 10:19 And the Cherubims lift vp their wings, and mounted vp from the earth in my sight: when they went out, the wheeles also were besides them, and euery one stood at the doore of the East gate of the Lords house, and the glorie of the God of Israel was ouer them aboue.
text.NABRE: 10:19 The cherubim lifted their wings and rose up from the earth before my eyes as they departed with the wheels beside them. They stopped at the entrance of the eastern gate of the Lord ’s house, and the glory of the God of Israel was up above them.

text.NRSV: These were the living creatures that I saw underneath the God of Israel by the river Chebar; and I knew that they were cherubim.
text.Rotherham: The same, was the living one which I saw under the God of Israel by the river Chebar,- then knew I that cherubim, they were.
text.KJV: 10:20 This is the liuing creature that I saw vnder the God of Israel, by the riuer of Chebar, and I knew that they were the Cherubims.
text.NABRE: 10:20 These were the living creatures I had seen beneath the God of Israel by the river Chebar. Now I knew they were cherubim.

text.NRSV: Each had four faces, and each four wings, and underneath their wings something like human hands.
text.Rotherham: Four faces severally, had each one, and four wings, had each one,—and the likeness of the hands of a man, was under their wings.
text.KJV: 10:21 Euery one had foure faces a piece, and euery one foure wings, and the likenesse of the handes of a man was vnder their wings.
text.NABRE: 10:21 Each of them had four faces and four wings, and something like human hands under their wings.

text.NRSV: As for the appearance of their faces, they were the very faces whose appearance I had seen by the river Chebar. Each one moved straight ahead.
text.Rotherham: And as for the likeness of their faces, They were the faces which I saw by the river Chebar, their appearances and themselves— every one straight before him, did they go.
text.KJV: 10:22 And the likenesse of their faces was the same faces which I saw by the riuer of Chebar, their appearances and themselues: they went euery one straight forward.
text.NABRE: 10:22 Their faces looked just like the faces I had seen by the river Chebar; and each one went straight ahead.

text.NRSV: The spirit lifted me up and brought me to the east gate of the house of the Lord, which faces east. There, at the entrance of the gateway, were twenty-five men; among them I saw Jaazaniah son of Azzur, and Pelatiah son of Benaiah, officials of the people.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit lifted me up, and brought me into the east gate of the house of Yahweh which looketh eastward, and lo! in the opening of the gate, twenty-five men,—and I saw in their midst Jaazaniah son of Azzur and Pelatiah son of Benaiah princes of the people.
text.KJV: 11:1 Moreouer the Spirit lift me vp, and brought me vnto the East gate of the Lords house, which looketh Eastward: and behold at the doore of the gate fiue and twenty men; among whom I saw Iaazaniah the sonne of Azur, and Pelatiah the sonne of Benaiah, Princes of the people.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 Death for the Remnant in Jerusalem. The spirit lifted me up and brought me to the east gate of the house of the Lord facing east. There at the entrance of the gate were twenty-five men; among them I saw the public officials Jaazaniah, son of Azzur, and Pelatiah, son of Benaiah.

text.NRSV: He said to me, “Mortal, these are the men who devise iniquity and who give wicked counsel in this city;
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto me, Son of man, These, are the men who are devising iniquity, and who are counselling wicked counsel in this city;
text.KJV: 11:2 Then said he vnto me; Sonne of man, these are the men that deuise mischiefe, & giue wicked counsel in this city.
text.NABRE: 11:2 The Lord said to me: Son of man, these are the men who are planning evil and giving wicked counsel in this city.

text.NRSV: they say, ‘The time is not near to build houses; this city is the pot, and we are the meat.’
text.Rotherham: who are saying, Not near! let us build houses,— It, is the caldron, and, we, are the flesh!
text.KJV: 11:3 Which say, It is not neere, let vs build houses: this citie is the caldron, and we be the flesh.
text.NABRE: 11:3 They are saying, “No need to build houses! The city is the pot, and we are the meat.”

text.NRSV: Therefore prophesy against them; prophesy, O mortal.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, prophesy against them, —prophesy. Son of man.
text.KJV: 11:4 ¶ Therefore prophecie against them, prophecie, O sonne of man.
text.NABRE: 11:4 Therefore prophesy against them, son of man, prophesy!

text.NRSV: Then the spirit of the Lord fell upon me, and he said to me, “Say, Thus says the Lord: This is what you think, O house of Israel; I know the things that come into your mind.
text.Rotherham: Then fell upon me the Spirit of Yahweh, and he said unto me Say—Thus, saith Yahweh, Thus have ye said O house of Israel, Yea, the things that come up on your spirit, I, know every one.
text.KJV: 11:5 And the Spirit of the Lord fell vpon me, and said vnto me, Speake, thus saith the Lord; Thus haue ye said, O house of Israel: for I know the things that come into your minde, euery one of them.
text.NABRE: 11:5 Then the spirit of the Lord fell upon me and told me to say: Thus says the Lord: This is how you talk, house of Israel. I know the things that come into your mind!

text.NRSV: You have killed many in this city, and have filled its streets with the slain.
text.Rotherham: Ye have multiplied your slain in this city,- And have filled her streets with slain.
text.KJV: 11:6 Ye haue multiplyed your slaine in this citie, and yee haue filled the streetes thereof with the slaine.
text.NABRE: 11:6 You have slain many in this city, filled its streets with the slain.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: The slain whom you have placed within it are the meat, and this city is the pot; but you shall be taken out of it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Your slain. whom ye have laid in her midst, They are the flesh, and She is the caldron,—when ye are taken out of her midst.
text.KJV: 11:7 Therefore thus sayth the Lord God; Your slaine whom ye haue laid in the middest of it, they are the flesh, and this citie is the cauldron: but I wil bring you foorth out of the middest of it.
text.NABRE: 11:7 Therefore thus says the Lord God: The slain whom you piled up in it, that is the meat, the pot is the city. But you I will bring out of it.

text.NRSV: You have feared the sword; and I will bring the sword upon you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: A sword, have ye feared,— And a sword, will I bring in upon you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:8 Ye haue feared the sword, and I will bring a sword vpon you, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 11:8 You fear the sword—that sword I will bring upon you—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: I will take you out of it and give you over to the hands of foreigners, and execute judgments upon you.
text.Rotherham: So will I take you forth out of her midst, and will deliver you into the hand of foreigners, —and will execute upon you judgments.
text.KJV: 11:9 And I will bring you out of the middest thereof, and deliuer you into the hands of strangers, and will execute iudgements among you.
text.NABRE: 11:9 I will bring you out of the city, hand you over to foreigners, and execute judgments against you.

text.NRSV: You shall fall by the sword; I will judge you at the border of Israel. And you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: By the sword, shall ye fall, Upon the boundary of Israel, will I judge you, —So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:10 Yee shall fall by the sword, I will iudge you in the border of Israel, and ye shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 11:10 By the sword you shall fall. At the borders of Israel I will judge you so that you will know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: This city shall not be your pot, and you shall not be the meat inside it; I will judge you at the border of Israel.
text.Rotherham: This city shall not serve you for a caldron, Neither shall, ye, serve in the midst thereof for flesh,—Near the boundary of Israel, will I judge you,
text.KJV: 11:11 This citie shall not be your cauldron, neither shall ye be the flesh in the middest thereof, but I will iudge you in the border of Israel.
text.NABRE: 11:11 The city shall not be a pot for you, nor shall you be meat within it. At the borders of Israel I will judge you,

text.NRSV: Then you shall know that I am the Lord, whose statutes you have not followed, and whose ordinances you have not kept, but you have acted according to the ordinances of the nations that are around you."
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh. In whose statutes, ye have not walked And whose appointments, ye have not executed,— But according to the appointments of the nations that are round about you, have ye done.
text.KJV: 11:12 And ye shall know that I am the Lord: for yee haue not walked in my statutes, neither executed my iudgements, but haue done after the maners of the heathen that are round about you.
text.NABRE: 11:12 so you shall know that I am the Lord, whose statutes you did not follow, whose ordinances you did not keep. Instead, you acted according to the ordinances of the nations around you.

text.NRSV: Now while I was prophesying, Pelatiah son of Benaiah died. Then I fell down on my face, cried with a loud voice, and said, “Ah, Lord God! will you make a full end of the remnant of Israel?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass as I prophesied, that Pelatiah son of Benaiah died,—so then I fell down upon my face and made outcry with a loud voice and said— Alas! My Lord Yahweh! A full end, art thou making of the remnant of Israel?
text.KJV: 11:13 ¶ And it came to passe, when I prophecied, that Pelatiah the sonne of Benaiah died: then fell I downe vpon my face, and cried with a loud voice, and said; Ah Lord God, wilt thou make a full end of the remnant of Israel?
text.NABRE: 11:13 While I was prophesying, Pelatiah, the son of Benaiah, dropped dead. I fell down on my face and cried out in a loud voice: “Alas, Lord God! You are finishing off what remains of Israel!”

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:
text.KJV: 11:14 Againe the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: 11:14 Restoration for the Exiles. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, your kinsfolk, your own kin, your fellow exiles, the whole house of Israel, all of them, are those of whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, “They have gone far from the Lord; to us this land is given for a possession.”
text.Rotherham: Son of man Thine own brethren, thine own brethren the men of thy kindred, even all the house of Israel all of it are they to whom the inhabitants of Jerusalem have said, Get you far away from Yahweh, To us, it is that the land, hath been given, for a possession!
text.KJV: 11:15 Sonne of man, thy brethren, euen thy brethren, the men of thy kinred, and all the house of Israel wholly are they, vnto whom the inhabitants of Ierusalem haue sayd; Get yee farre from the Lord: vnto vs is this land giuen in possession.
text.NABRE: 11:15 Son of man, the inhabitants of Jerusalem are saying about all your relatives, the other exiles, and all the house of Israel, “They are far away from the Lord. The land is given to us as a possession.”

text.NRSV: Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: Though I removed them far away among the nations, and though I scattered them among the countries, yet I have been a sanctuary to them for a little while in the countries where they have gone.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, say, Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Although I have removed them far away among the nations, And although I have dispersed them throughout the lands Yet have I become to them a sanctuary for a little while, in the lands which they have entered
text.KJV: 11:16 Therefore say, Thus sayth the Lord God; Although I haue cast them farre off among the heathen, and although I haue scattered them among the countreys, yet will I be to them as a little Sanctuarie in the countreys where they shall come.
text.NABRE: 11:16 Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: I have indeed sent them far away among the nations, scattered them over the lands, and have been but little sanctuary for them in the lands to which they have gone.

text.NRSV: Therefore say: Thus says the Lord God: I will gather you from the peoples, and assemble you out of the countries where you have been scattered, and I will give you the land of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Therefore say, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Therefore will I assemble you from among the peoples, And gather you out of the lands wherein ye have been dispersed,- And will give you the soil of Israel.
text.KJV: 11:17 Therefore say, Thus saith the Lord God; I will euen gather you from the people, and assemble you out of the countreys where ye haue beene scattered, and I will giue you the land of Israel.
text.NABRE: 11:17 Therefore, thus says the Lord God, I will gather you from the nations and collect you from the lands through which you were scattered, so I can give you the land of Israel.

text.NRSV: When they come there, they will remove from it all its detestable things and all its abominations.
text.Rotherham: So shall they come in thither,— and take away all her detestable things, and all her abominations, out of her;
text.KJV: 11:18 And they shall come thither, and they shall take away all the detestable things thereof, and all the abominations thereof from thence.
text.NABRE: 11:18 They will enter it and remove all its atrocities and abominations.

text.NRSV: I will give them one heart, and put a new spirit within them; I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh,
text.Rotherham: And I will give them another heart, And a new spirit, will I put within you,—And will take away the heart of stone out of their flesh, and give them a heart of flesh:
text.KJV: 11:19 And I wil giue them one heart, and I wil put a new spirit within you: and I will take the stonie heart out of their flesh, and will giue them an heart of flesh,
text.NABRE: 11:19 And I will give them another heart and a new spirit I will put within them. From their bodies I will remove the hearts of stone, and give them hearts of flesh,

text.NRSV: so that they may follow my statutes and keep my ordinances and obey them. Then they shall be my people, and I will be their God.
text.Rotherham: To the end that in my statutes, they may walk, And mine appointments, they may keep, and do them,— So shall they become my people, And I will become their God.
text.KJV: 11:20 That they may walke in my statutes, and keepe mine ordinances, and doe them: and they shall be my people, and I will be their God.
text.NABRE: 11:20 so that they walk according to my statutes, taking care to keep my ordinances. Thus they will be my people, and I will be their God.

text.NRSV: But as for those whose heart goes after their detestable things and their abominations, I will bring their deeds upon their own heads, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: But as for them who having a heart for their detestable things and for their abominations after their own heart, do walk, Their way upon their own heads, will I render, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:21 But as for them whose heart walketh after the heart of their detestable things, and their abominations, I wil recompense their way vpon their owne heads, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 11:21 But as for those whose hearts are devoted to their atrocities and abominations, I will bring their conduct down upon their heads—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: Then the cherubim lifted up their wings, with the wheels beside them; and the glory of the God of Israel was above them.
text.Rotherham: Then did the cherubim lift up their wings, the wheels also going in unison with them,— the glory of the God of Israel, being over them above.
text.KJV: 11:22 ¶ Then did the Cherubims lift vp their wings, and the wheeles besides them, and the glory of the God of Israel was ouer them aboue.
text.NABRE: 11:22 Then the cherubim lifted their wings and the wheels alongside them, with the glory of the God of Israel above them.

text.NRSV: And the glory of the Lord ascended from the middle of the city, and stopped on the mountain east of the city.
text.Rotherham: Thus went up the glory of Yahweh, out from the midst of the city,—and stood on the mountain, which is on the east of the city.
text.KJV: 11:23 And the glory of the Lord went vp from the middest of the citie, and stood vpon the mountaine, which is on the East side of the citie.
text.NABRE: 11:23 The glory of the Lord rose up from the middle of the city and came to rest on the mountain east of the city.

text.NRSV: The spirit lifted me up and brought me in a vision by the spirit of God into Chaldea, to the exiles. Then the vision that I had seen left me.
text.Rotherham: And, the spirit lifted me up, and brought me to Chaldea unto them of the captivity, in the vision by the Spirit of God,—thus went up from me, the vision which I had seen.
text.KJV: 11:24 ¶ Afterwards the spirit tooke me vp, and brought me in vision by the spirit of God into Caldea to them of the captiuity: so the vision that I had seene, went vp from me.
text.NABRE: 11:24 In a vision, the spirit lifted me up and brought me back to the exiles in Chaldea, by the spirit of God. The vision I had seen left me,

text.NRSV: And I told the exiles all the things that the Lord had shown me.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto them of the captivity,—all the words of Yahweh which he had shewed me.
text.KJV: 11:25 Then I spake vnto them of the captiuity, all the things that the Lord had shewed me.
text.NABRE: 11:25 and I told the exiles everything the Lord had shown me.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:
text.KJV: 12:1 The word of the Lord also came vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 Acts Symbolic of the Exile. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, you are living in the midst of a rebellious house, who have eyes to see but do not see, who have ears to hear but do not hear;
text.Rotherham: Son of man. In the midst of a perverse house, dost thou dwell,— Who have eyes to see—and have not seen. Ears have they to hear—and have no heard, For a perverse house, they are.
text.KJV: 12:2 Sonne of man, thou dwellest in the middest of a rebellious house, which haue eyes to see, and see not: they haue eares to heare, and heare not: for they are a rebellious house.
text.NABRE: 12:2 Son of man, you live in the midst of a rebellious house; they have eyes to see, but do not see, and ears to hear but do not hear. They are such a rebellious house!

text.NRSV: for they are a rebellious house. Therefore, mortal, prepare for yourself an exile’s baggage, and go into exile by day in their sight; you shall go like an exile from your place to another place in their sight. Perhaps they will understand, though they are a rebellious house.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore Son of man, Prepare thee baggage for exile, and exile thyself, by day, before their eyes,—so shalt thou exile thyself out of thy place unto another place before their eyes, peradventure they will consider— though a perverse house, they are.
text.KJV: 12:3 Therefore thou sonne of man, prepare thee stuffe for remoouing, and remooue by day in their sight, and thou shalt remoue from thy place to another place in their sight; it may be they will consider, though they bee a rebellious house.
text.NABRE: 12:3 Now, son of man, during the day while they watch, pack a bag for exile, and again while they watch, go into exile from your place to another place; perhaps they will see that they are a rebellious house.

text.NRSV: You shall bring out your baggage by day in their sight, as baggage for exile; and you shall go out yourself at evening in their sight, as those do who go into exile.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou take forth thy baggage as baggage for exile, by day before their eyes,—and thou thyself, shalt go forth in the evening, before their eyes, like them who go forth to exile.
text.KJV: 12:4 Then shalt thou bring foorth thy stuffe by day in their sight, as stuffe for remoouing: and thou shalt goe foorth at euen in their sight, as they that goe foorth into captiuitie.
text.NABRE: 12:4 During the day, while they watch, bring out your bag, an exile’s bag. In the evening, again while they watch, go out as if into exile.

text.NRSV: Dig through the wall in their sight, and carry the baggage out through it.
text.Rotherham: Before their eyes, break thou forth by thyself through the wall,—and carry forth through it.
text.KJV: 12:5 Digge thou through the wall in their sight, and cary out thereby.
text.NABRE: 12:5 While they watch, dig a hole through the wall and go out through it.

text.NRSV: In their sight you shall lift the baggage upon your shoulder, and carry it out in the dark; you shall cover your face, so that you may not see the land; for I have made you a sign for the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Before their eyes, Upon the shoulder, shalt thou lift it. In thick darkness, take it forth, Thy face, shalt thou cover so that thou see not the land; For a sign, have I appointed thee to the house of Israel.
text.KJV: 12:6 In their sight shalt thou beare it vpon thy shoulders, and cary it foorth in the twy light: thou shalt couer thy face, that thou see not the ground: for I haue set thee for a signe vnto the house of Israel.
text.NABRE: 12:6 While they watch, shoulder your load and go out in darkness. Cover your face so you cannot see the land, for I am making you a sign for the house of Israel!

text.NRSV: I did just as I was commanded. I brought out my baggage by day, as baggage for exile, and in the evening I dug through the wall with my own hands; I brought it out in the dark, carrying it on my shoulder in their sight.
text.Rotherham: And I did so, just as I was commanded, My baggage, took I forth as baggage for exile by day, and in the evening, I brake forth by myself through the wall, by force: in the twilight, I took it forth—on to my shoulder, I lifted it, before their eyes.
text.KJV: 12:7 And I did so as I was commanded: I brought forth my stuffe by day, as stuffe for captiuity, and in the euen I digged through the wall with mine hand, I brought it foorth in the twy light, and I bare it vpon my shoulder in their sight.
text.NABRE: 12:7 I did just as I was commanded. During the day I brought out my bag, an exile’s bag. In the evening while they watched, I dug a hole through the wall with my hands and set out in darkness, shouldering my load.

text.NRSV: In the morning the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me in the morning, saying:
text.KJV: 12:8 ¶ And in the morning came the word of the Lord vnto me, saying,
text.NABRE: 12:8 In the morning, the word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, has not the house of Israel, the rebellious house, said to you, “What are you doing?”
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Have not the house of Israel the perverse house, said unto thee,— What art thou doing?
text.KJV: 12:9 Sonne of man, hath not the house of Israel, the rebellious house, sayd vnto thee, What doest thou?
text.NABRE: 12:9 Son of man, did not the house of Israel, that house of rebels, say, “What are you doing?”

text.NRSV: Say to them, “Thus says the Lord God: This oracle concerns the prince in Jerusalem and all the house of Israel in it.”
text.Rotherham: Say unto them, Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh,— [For] the Bearer, is this burden, in Jerusalem, and [for] all the house of Israel such as are in their midst.
text.KJV: 12:10 Say thou vnto them; Thus saith the Lord God; This burden concerneth the Prince in Ierusalem, and all the house of Israel that are among them.
text.NABRE: 12:10 Tell them: Thus says the Lord God: This load is the prince in Jerusalem and the whole house of Israel within it.

text.NRSV: Say, “I am a sign for you: as I have done, so shall it be done to them; they shall go into exile, into captivity.”
text.Rotherham: Say, I, am your sign: As I have done, so. shall it be done to them, Into exile—into captivity, shall they go.
text.KJV: 12:11 Say, I am your signe: like as I haue done, so shall it be done vnto them: they shall remooue and goe into captiuitie.
text.NABRE: 12:11 Say, I am a sign for you: just as I have done, so it shall be done to them; into exile, as captives they shall go.

text.NRSV: And the prince who is among them shall lift his baggage on his shoulder in the dark, and shall go out; he shall dig through the wall to carry it out through it; he shall cover his face, so that he may not see the land with his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the Bearer who is in their midst, Upon his shoulder, shall lift it. In thick darkness, shall he go forth, Through the wall, shall they break to bear forth through it,— His face, shall he cover, to the end that, his own eye, may not see the land.
text.KJV: 12:12 And the Prince that is among them, shall beare vpon his shoulder in the twylight, and shall goe forth: they shall digge through the wall to cary out thereby: he shall couer his face, that he see not the ground with his eyes.
text.NABRE: 12:12 The prince among them shall shoulder his load in darkness and go out through the hole they dug in the wall to bring him out. His face shall be covered so that he cannot even see the ground.

text.NRSV: I will spread my net over him, and he shall be caught in my snare; and I will bring him to Babylon, the land of the Chaldeans, yet he shall not see it; and he shall die there.
text.Rotherham: Then will I spread my net over him, and he shall be taken in my snare; And I will take him to Babylon. in the land of the Chaldeans, The which, indeed, he shall not see. And yet there, shall he die!
text.KJV: 12:13 My net also will I spread vpon him, and he shall be taken in my snare, and I wil bring him to Babylon to the land of the Caldeans, yet shall hee not see it, though he shall die there.
text.NABRE: 12:13 I will spread my net over him and he shall be caught in my snare. I will bring him into Babylon, to the land of the Chaldeans, though he shall not see it, and there he shall die.

text.NRSV: I will scatter to every wind all who are around him, his helpers and all his troops; and I will unsheathe the sword after them.
text.Rotherham: And all who are round about him to help him and all his troops, will I scatter to every wind,—and, a sword, will I make bare after them.
text.KJV: 12:14 And I will scatter toward euery winde all that are about him to helpe him, and all his bands, and I wil draw out the sword after them.
text.NABRE: 12:14 All his retinue, his aides and all his troops, I will scatter to the winds and pursue them with the sword.

text.NRSV: And they shall know that I am the Lord, when I disperse them among the nations and scatter them through the countries.
text.Rotherham: So shall they know that, I, am Yahweh,— By my dispersing them among the nations, And scattering them throughout the lands.
text.KJV: 12:15 And they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shal scatter them among the nations, and disperse them in the countreys.
text.NABRE: 12:15 Then they shall know that I am the Lord, when I disperse them among the nations and scatter them throughout the lands.

text.NRSV: But I will let a few of them escape from the sword, from famine and pestilence, so that they may tell of all their abominations among the nations where they go; then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I leave remaining of them men easily counted, from the sword from the famine and from the pestilence,—that they may recount all their abominations among the nations whither they have come, So shall they know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 12:16 But I will leaue a few men of them from the sword, from the famine, and from the pestilence, that they may declare all their abominations among the heathen whither they come, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 12:16 But I will let a few of them escape the sword, starvation, and plague, so that they may recount all their abominations among the nations to which they go. Thus they may know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 12:17 ¶ Moreouer, the worde of the Lord came to me, saying;
text.NABRE: 12:17 The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, eat your bread with quaking, and drink your water with trembling and with fearfulness;
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Thy bread with trembling, shalt thou eat,— And, thy water in agitation and in fear, shalt thou drink.
text.KJV: 12:18 Sonne of man, eate thy bread with quaking, and drinke thy water with trembling and with carefulnesse,
text.NABRE: 12:18 Son of man, eat your bread trembling and drink your water shaking with fear.

text.NRSV: and say to the people of the land, Thus says the Lord God concerning the inhabitants of Jerusalem in the land of Israel: They shall eat their bread with fearfulness, and drink their water in dismay, because their land shall be stripped of all it contains, on account of the violence of all those who live in it.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say unto the people of the land, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh concerning the inhabitants of Jerusalem upon the soil of Israel, Their bread with anxious care, shall they eat, And, their water in astonishment, shall they drink,— That her land, may be deserted, of her fulness, because of the violence of all them who dwell therein:
