text.NRSV: As for your sisters, Sodom and her daughters shall return to their former state, Samaria and her daughters shall return to their former state, and you and your daughters shall return to your former state.
text.Rotherham: When thy sisters Sodom and her daughters shall return to their former estate, And Samaria and her daughters shall return to their former estate Then thou and thy daughters, shall return to your former estate.
text.KJV: 16:55 When thy sisters, Sodom and her daughters shal returne to their former estate, and Samaria and her daughters shall returne to their former estate, then thou and thy daughters shall returne to your former estate.
text.NABRE: 16:55 Yes, your sisters, Sodom and her daughters, Samaria and her daughters, shall return to the way they were, and you and your daughters shall return to the way you were.

text.NRSV: Was not your sister Sodom a byword in your mouth in the day of your pride,
text.Rotherham: And Sodom thy sister was never heard in thy mouth, —In the day of thy pride:
text.KJV: 16:56 For thy sister Sodom was not mentioned by thy mouth in the day of thy pride;
text.NABRE: 16:56 Did you not hold your sister Sodom in bad repute while you felt proud of yourself,

text.NRSV: before your wickedness was uncovered? Now you are a mockery to the daughters of Aram and all her neighbors, and to the daughters of the Philistines, those all around who despise you.
text.Rotherham: Before thy wickedness was discovered, As now, [thou art] the reproach of the daughters of Syria and all round about her the daughters of the Philistines, —who are despising thee on every side.
text.KJV: 16:57 Before thy wickednesse was discouered, as at the time of thy reproch of the daughters of Syria, and all that are round about her, the daughters of the Philistines which despise thee round about.
text.NABRE: 16:57 before your evil was exposed? Now you are like her, reproached by the Arameans and all their neighbors, despised on all sides by the Philistines.

text.NRSV: You must bear the penalty of your lewdness and your abominations, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: As for thy crime and thine abominations, thou thyself, dost bear them,— Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: 16:58 Thou hast borne thy lewdnesse, and thine abominations, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:58 The penalty of your depravity and your abominations—you must bear it all—oracle of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Yes, thus says the Lord God: I will deal with you as you have done, you who have despised the oath, breaking the covenant;
text.Rotherham: For Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, Therefore will I deal with thee just as thou hast dealt,- In that thou didst despise an oath by breaking a covenant.
text.KJV: 16:59 For thus saith the Lord God; I will euen deale with thee as thou hast done, which hast despised the oath in breaking the couenant.
text.NABRE: 16:59 For thus says the Lord God: I will deal with you for what you did; you despised an oath by breaking a covenant.

text.NRSV: yet I will remember my covenant with you in the days of your youth, and I will establish with you an everlasting covenant.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth,— And will establish for thee a covenant age-abiding.
text.KJV: 16:60 ¶ Neuerthelesse I will remember my couenant with thee in the dayes of thy youth, and I will establish vnto thee an euerlasting couenant.
text.NABRE: 16:60 But I will remember the covenant I made with you when you were young; I will set up an everlasting covenant with you.

text.NRSV: Then you will remember your ways, and be ashamed when I take your sisters, both your elder and your younger, and give them to you as daughters, but not on account of my covenant with you.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt therefore remember thy ways and take to thyself reproach, by receiving thy sisters. The older than thou, And the younger than thou,- And I will give them unto thee for daughters Though not by thine own covenant.
text.KJV: 16:61 Then thou shalt remember thy wayes and be ashamed, when thou shalt receiue thy sisters, thine elder and thy younger, and I will giue them vnto thee for daughters, but not by thy couenant.
text.NABRE: 16:61 Then you shall remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your sisters, those older and younger than you; I give them to you as daughters, but not by reason of your covenant.

text.NRSV: I will establish my covenant with you, and you shall know that I am the Lord,
text.Rotherham: But I myself, will establish my covenant with thee— And thou shalt know that I, am Yahweh:
text.KJV: 16:62 And I will establish my couenant with thee, and thou shalt know that I am the Lord:
text.NABRE: 16:62 For I will re-establish my covenant with you, that you may know that I am the Lord,

text.NRSV: in order that you may remember and be confounded, and never open your mouth again because of your shame, when I forgive you all that you have done, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: To the end thou mayest remember and turn pale, and there be to thee no more, an opening of mouth, because of thy reproach,- In that I have accepted a propitiatory covering for thee as to all that thou hast done, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh.
text.KJV: 16:63 That thou mayest remember, and bee confounded, and neuer open thy mouth any more: because of thy shame, when I am pacified toward thee, for all that thou hast done, sayeth the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 16:63 that you may remember and be ashamed, and never again open your mouth because of your disgrace, when I pardon you for all you have done—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: 17:1 And the word of the Lord came vnto mee, saying,
text.NABRE: 17:1 Chapter 17 The Eagles and the Vine. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, propound a riddle, and speak an allegory to the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Put thou forth a riddle And speak thou a parable,- Unto the house of Israel:
text.KJV: 17:2 Sonne of man, put foorth a riddle, and speake a parable vnto the house of Israel,
text.NABRE: 17:2 Son of man, propose a riddle, and tell this proverb to the house of Israel:

text.NRSV: say: Thus says the Lord God: A great eagle, with great wings and long pinions, rich in plumage of many colors, came to the Lebanon. He took the top of the cedar,
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt say, Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh,— A great eagle, with large wings of long pinion, full of plumage, which had divers colours, came unto Lebanon, and took the highest branch of the cedar:
text.KJV: 17:3 And say, Thus saith the Lord God, A great eagle with great wings, long wing`d, full of feathers, which had diuers colours, camevnto Lebanon, and tooke the highest branch of the Cedar.
text.NABRE: 17:3 Thus says the Lord God: The great eagle, with wide wingspan and long feathers, with thick plumage, many-hued, came to Lebanon. He plucked the crest of the cedar,

text.NRSV: broke off its topmost shoot; he carried it to a land of trade, and set it in a city of merchants.
text.Rotherham: The crown of its young twigs, plucked he off, and brought it into a land of traffic, in a city of merchants, he set it.
text.KJV: 17:4 Hee cropt off the top of his yong twigs, and caried it into a land of traffique; he set it in a city of merchants.
text.NABRE: 17:4 broke off its topmost branch, And brought it to a land of merchants, set it in a city of traders.

text.NRSV: Then he took a seed from the land, placed it in fertile soil; A plant by abundant waters, he set it like a willow twig.
text.Rotherham: So he took of the seed of the land, and planted it in a field for seed,- He took it near mighty waters, Like a willow-tree, he set it.
text.KJV: 17:5 Hee tooke also of the seed of the land, and planted it in a fruitfull field, he placed it by great waters, and set it as a willow tree.
text.NABRE: 17:5 Then he took some native seed and planted it in fertile soil; A shoot beside plentiful waters, like a willow he planted it,

text.NRSV: It sprouted and became a low-spreading vine, and its branches turned toward him, and its roots remained where it was. So it became a vine; it brought forth branches and put forth foliage.
text.Rotherham: And it shot forth, and became a spreading vine of lowly stature, Its branches turning towards him, And its roots under him spread,- So it became a vine, And brought forth shoots, And sent forth boughs.
text.KJV: 17:6 And it grew, and became a spreading Uine of low stature, whose branches turned toward him, and the roots thereof were vnder him: so it became a Uine, and brought forth branches, and shot foorth sprigges.
text.NABRE: 17:6 That it might sprout and become a vine, dense and low-lying, With its branches turned toward him, its roots beneath it. Thus it became a vine, produced branches, and put forth shoots.

text.NRSV: There was another great eagle, with great wings and much plumage. And see! This vine stretched out its roots toward him; it shot out its branches toward him from the bed where it was planted so that he might water it.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that there was another great eagle, with large wings, and abundant plumage,- And lo! this vine, stretched its roots hungry towards him. And its branches, sent it forth unto him, That he might cause it to drink out of the beds where it was planted:
text.KJV: 17:7 There was also an other great eagle, with great wings and many feathers, and behold, this Uine did bend her rootes towards him, and shot forth her branches toward him, that hee might water it by the furrowes of her plantation.
text.NABRE: 17:7 Then another great eagle appeared, with wide wingspan, rich in plumage, And see! This vine bent its roots to him, sent out branches for him to water. From the bed where it was planted,

text.NRSV: It was transplanted to good soil by abundant waters, so that it might produce branches and bear fruit and become a noble vine.
text.Rotherham: In a goodly field by mighty waters, was it planted,- That it might bring forth boughs, and bear fruit, That it might become a splendid vine.
text.KJV: 17:8 It was planted in a good soile by great waters, that it might bring forth branches, and that it might beare fruit, that it might be a goodly Uine.
text.NABRE: 17:8 it was transplanted to a fertile field By abundant waters, to produce branches, to bear fruit, to become a majestic vine.

text.NRSV: Say: Thus says the Lord God: Will it prosper? Will he not pull up its roots and cause its fruit to rot and wither, its fresh sprouting leaves to fade? No great power or many people will be needed to pull it from its roots.
text.Rotherham: Say thou, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Shall it thrive? Its roots, will he not tear up? Its fruit, will he not cut off, that it wither? All the fresh leaves it hath caused to spring forth shall wither without a strong arm or many people, to pull it up by its roots.
text.KJV: 17:9 Say thou, Thus saith the Lord God; Shall it prosper? shall he not pull vp the rootes thereof, and cut off the fruit thereof, that it wither? it shall wither in all the leaues of her spring, euen without great power, or many people to plucke it vp by the rootes thereof.
text.NABRE: 17:9 Say: Thus says the Lord God: Can it thrive? Will he not tear up its roots and strip its fruit? Then all its green leaves will wither— neither strong arm nor mighty nation is needed to uproot it.

text.NRSV: Even if it is transplanted, will it prosper? Will it not utterly wither when the east wind strikes it— wither away on the bed where it grew?
text.Rotherham: Lo! therefore though [it remain] planted, shall it thrive? As soon as an east wind toucheth it, will it not utterly wither? On the beds where it sprang up, will it not wither?
text.KJV: 17:10 Yea behold, being planted, shall it prosper? shall it not vtterly wither, when the East wind toucheth it? it shal wither in the furrowes where it grew.
text.NABRE: 17:10 True, it is planted; but will it thrive? Will it not wither up When the east wind strikes it, wither in the very bed where it sprouted?

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:
text.KJV: 17:11 ¶ Moreouer the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying,
text.NABRE: 17:11 The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Say now to the rebellious house: Do you not know what these things mean? Tell them: The king of Babylon came to Jerusalem, took its king and its officials, and brought them to him in Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Say, I pray thee unto the perverse house, Know ye not what these things are? Say thou Lo! the King of Babylon entered Jerusalem And took her king and her princes, And brought them unto him in Babylon;
text.KJV: 17:12 Say now to the rebellious house, Know ye not what these things meane? tell them, behold, the king of Babylon is come to Ierusalem, and hath taken the King thereof, and the Princes thereof, and ledde them with him to Babylon,
text.NABRE: 17:12 Now say to the rebellious house: Do you not understand this? Tell them! The king of Babylon came to Jerusalem and took away its king and officials and brought them to him in Babylon.

text.NRSV: He took one of the royal offspring and made a covenant with him, putting him under oath (he had taken away the chief men of the land),
text.Rotherham: Yea took of the seed royal And solemnised with him a covenant, And brought him into an oath, Also the mighty ones of the land, did he take.
text.KJV: 17:13 And hath taken of the kings seed, and made a couenant with him, and hath taken an oath of him: he hath also taken the mighty of the land,
text.NABRE: 17:13 After removing the nobles from the land, he then took one of the royal line And made a covenant with him, binding him under oath,

text.NRSV: so that the kingdom might be humble and not lift itself up, and that by keeping his covenant it might stand.
text.Rotherham: That, the kingdom might be abased, so as not to lift itself up,— By the keeping of his covenant, might be made to stand.
text.KJV: 17:14 That the kingdome might bee base, that it might not lift it selfe vp, but that by keeping of his Couenant it might stand.
text.NABRE: 17:14 To be a humble kingdom, without high aspirations, to keep his covenant and so survive.

text.NRSV: But he rebelled against him by sending ambassadors to Egypt, that they might give him horses and a large army. Will he succeed? Can one escape who does such things? Can he break the covenant and yet escape?
text.Rotherham: But he hath rebelled against him by sending his messengers to Egypt, that there should be given to him horses, and much people. Shall he thrive? Shall he escape that doeth these things? Shall he break a covenant and escape?
text.KJV: 17:15 But he rebelled against him in sending his ambassadours into Egypt, that they might giue him horses and much people: shall he prosper? shall he escape that doeth such things? or shall hee breake the Couenant, and bee deliuered?
text.NABRE: 17:15 But this one rebelled against him by sending envoys to Egypt To obtain horses and a mighty army. Can he thrive? Can he escape if he does this? Can he break a covenant and go free?

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord God, surely in the place where the king resides who made him king, whose oath he despised, and whose covenant he broke—in Babylon he shall die.
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Very! in the place where dwelleth the king that made him king, Whose oath he hath despised, And whose covenant he hath broken With him in the midst of Babylon, shall he die.
text.KJV: 17:16 As I liue, saith the Lord God, surely in the place where the king dwelleth that made him king, whose oath he despised, and whose couenant he brake, euen with him, in the midst of Babylon he shall die.
text.NABRE: 17:16 As I live—oracle of the Lord God — in the house of the king who set him up to rule, Whose oath he ignored and whose covenant he broke, there in Babylon I swear he shall die!

text.NRSV: Pharaoh with his mighty army and great company will not help him in war, when mounds are cast up and siege-walls built to cut off many lives.
text.Rotherham: Neither shall Pharaoh with a great force. or with a large gathered host, work with him in the war, by casting up an earthwork and by building a siege-wall, —to the cuting off of many lives.
text.KJV: 17:17 Neither shall Pharaoh with his mightie armie and great companie make for him in the warre by casting vp mounts, and building forts, to cut off many persons.
text.NABRE: 17:17 Pharaoh shall not help him on the day of battle, with a great force and mighty horde, When ramps are thrown up and siege works built for the cutting down of many lives.

text.NRSV: Because he despised the oath and broke the covenant—because he gave his hand and yet did all these things—he shall not escape.
text.Rotherham: Seeing he hath despised an oath. by breaking a covenant—yea lo! hath given his hand and all these things hath done he shall not escape.
text.KJV: 17:18 Seeing hee despised the oath by breaking the couenant (when loe, he had giuen his hand) and hath done all these things, he shall not escape.
text.NABRE: 17:18 He ignored his oath, breaking his covenant; even though he gave his hand, he did all these things— he shall not escape!

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: As I live, I will surely return upon his head my oath that he despised, and my covenant that he broke.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh As I live, Surely it is mine oath which he hath despised, and my covenant which he hath broken, Therefore will I bring it upon his own head:
text.KJV: 17:19 Therefore thus saith the Lord God, As I liue, surely mine oath that he hath despised, and my Couenant that he hath broken, euen it will I recompense vpon his owne head.
text.NABRE: 17:19 Therefore, thus says the Lord God: As I live, my oath which he spurned, And my covenant which he broke, I will bring down on his head.

text.NRSV: I will spread my net over him, and he shall be caught in my snare; I will bring him to Babylon and come into judgment with him there for the treason he has committed against me.
text.Rotherham: And I will spread over him my net, And he shall be taken in my snare, And I will bring him into Babylon and will enter into judgment with him there, as to his treachery wherewith he hath been treacherous against me;
text.KJV: 17:20 And I will spread my net vpon him, & he shalbe taken in my snare, and I will bring him to Babylon, and will plead with him there for his trespasse, that he hath trespassed against me.
text.NABRE: 17:20 I will spread my net over him, and he will be caught in my snare. I will bring him to Babylon to judge him there because he broke faith with me.

text.NRSV: All the pick of his troops shall fall by the sword, and the survivors shall be scattered to every wind; and you shall know that I, the Lord, have spoken.
text.Rotherham: And all his fugitives throughout all his bands by the sword, shall fall, And they who are left, to every wind, shall be scattered,- [So shall ye know that I, Yahweh, have spoken!]
text.KJV: 17:21 And all his fugitiues, with all his bands, shall fall by the sword, and they that remaine shalbe scattered towards all windes: and ye shall know that I the Lord haue spoken it.
text.NABRE: 17:21 Any among his forces who escape will fall by the sword, And whoever might survive will be scattered to the winds. Thus you will know that I the Lord have spoken.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: I myself will take a sprig from the lofty top of a cedar; I will set it out. I will break off a tender one from the topmost of its young twigs; I myself will plant it on a high and lofty mountain.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Therefore will I, Myself, take of the highest branch of the lofty cedar and set it,— Of the crown of its young twigs, a tender one, will I pluck off, and I. myself will plant it upon a mountain high and terraced,
text.KJV: 17:22 ¶ Thus saith the Lord God, I wil also take of the highest branch of the high Cedar, and will set it, I will croppe off from the top of his yong twigges a tender one, and will plant it vpon an high mountaine and eminent.
text.NABRE: 17:22 Thus says the Lord God: I, too, will pluck from the crest of the cedar the highest branch. From the top a tender shoot I will break off and transplant on a high, lofty mountain.

text.NRSV: On the mountain height of Israel I will plant it, in order that it may produce boughs and bear fruit, and become a noble cedar. Under it every kind of bird will live; in the shade of its branches will nest winged creatures of every kind.
text.Rotherham: In the mountain of the height of Israel, will I plant it, And it shall put forth boughs And bear fruit, And become a majestic cedar,- And every bird of every wing shall dwell under it, In the shade of its branches, shall they dwell.
text.KJV: 17:23 In the mountaine of the height of Israel will I plant it: and it shall bring foorth boughes, and beare fruite, and be a goodly Cedar, and vnder it shall dwell all foule of euery wing: in the shadow of the branches thereof shal they dwell.
text.NABRE: 17:23 On the mountain height of Israel I will plant it. It shall put forth branches and bear fruit, and become a majestic cedar. Every small bird will nest under it, all kinds of winged birds will dwell in the shade of its branches.

text.NRSV: All the trees of the field shall know that I am the Lord. I bring low the high tree, I make high the low tree; I dry up the green tree and make the dry tree flourish. I the Lord have spoken; I will do it.
text.Rotherham: So shall all the trees of the field know that I Yahweh Have laid low the high tree Have exalted the low tree, Have dried up the moist tree, And have caused to flourish the tree, that was dry,— I, Yahweh, have spoken, and performed.
text.KJV: 17:24 And all the trees of the field shall know that I the Lord haue brought downe the high tree, haue exalted the low tree, haue dried vp the greene tree, and haue made the drie tree to flourish: I the Lord haue spoken, and haue done it.
text.NABRE: 17:24 Every tree of the field will know that I am the Lord. I bring low the high tree, lift high the lowly tree, Wither up the green tree, and make the dry tree bloom. As I, the Lord, have spoken, so will I do!

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: 18:1 And the word of þ Lord came vnto me againe, saying;
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 Personal Responsibility. The word of the Lord came to me: Son of man,

text.NRSV: What do you mean by repeating this proverb concerning the land of Israel, “The parents have eaten sour grapes, and the children’s teeth are set on edge”?
text.Rotherham: What occasion have ye, to be using this proverb, concerning the soil of Israel, saying, —Fathers eat sour grapes, And the children’s teeth are blunted?
text.KJV: 18:2 What meane ye that yee vse this prouerbe concerning the land of Israel, saying, The fathers haue eaten sowre grapes, and the childrens teeth are set on edge?
text.NABRE: 18:2 what is the meaning of this proverb you recite in the land of Israel: “Parents eat sour grapes, but the children’s teeth are set on edge”?

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord God, this proverb shall no more be used by you in Israel.
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth My Lord, Yahweh, Surely ye shall have occasion no longer to use this proverb, in Israel.
text.KJV: 18:3 As I liue, saith the Lord God, yee shall not haue occasion any more to vse this prouerbe in Israel.
text.NABRE: 18:3 As I live—oracle of the Lord God: I swear that none of you will ever repeat this proverb in Israel.

text.NRSV: Know that all lives are mine; the life of the parent as well as the life of the child is mine: it is only the person who sins that shall die.
text.Rotherham: Lo! as for all persons, mine they are, As the person of the father, so also the person of the son, mine they are,—The person that sinneth, the same shall die.
text.KJV: 18:4 Behold, all soules are mine, as the soule of the father, so also the soule of the sonne is mine: the soule that sinneth, it shall die.
text.NABRE: 18:4 For all life is mine: the life of the parent is like the life of the child, both are mine. Only the one who sins shall die!

text.NRSV: If a man is righteous and does what is lawful and right—
text.Rotherham: But when, any man, shall be righteous, and do justice and righteousness:
text.KJV: 18:5 ¶ But if a man be iust, and do that which is lawfull and right:
text.NABRE: 18:5 If a man is just—if he does what is right,

text.NRSV: if he does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, does not defile his neighbor’s wife or approach a woman during her menstrual period,
text.Rotherham: Upon the mountains, hath not eaten, And his eyes, hath not lifted up unto the manufactured gods of the house of Israel, And the wife of his neighbour, hath not defiled, And unto a woman during her removal, hath not approached;
text.KJV: 18:6 And hath not eaten vpon the mountaines, neither hath lift vp his eyes to the idoles of the house of Israel, neither hath defiled his neighbours wife, neither hath come neere to a menstruous woman,
text.NABRE: 18:6 if he does not eat on the mountains, or raise his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel; if he does not defile a neighbor’s wife, or have relations with a woman during her period;

text.NRSV: does not oppress anyone, but restores to the debtor his pledge, commits no robbery, gives his bread to the hungry and covers the naked with a garment,
text.Rotherham: And no man, hath treated with violence, But his debt-pledge, hath restored. Plunder, hath not seized,— His bread—to the famished, hath given, And the naked, hath covered with clothing;
text.KJV: 18:7 And hath not oppressed any, but hath restored to the debtourhis pledge, hath spoiled none by violence, hath giuen his bread to the hungrie, and hath couered the naked with a garment,
text.NABRE: 18:7 if he oppresses no one, gives back the pledge received for a debt, commits no robbery; gives food to the hungry and clothes the naked;

text.NRSV: does not lend at interest or take any profit, withholds his hand from injustice, executes true justice between contending parties,
text.Rotherham: Upon interest, hath not put out his money. And increase, hath not accepted, From dishonesty, hath turned back his hand,— Justice in truth, hath done, between man and man;
text.KJV: 18:8 He that hath not giuen foorth vpon vsurie, neither hath taken any increase, that hath withdrawen his hand from iniquitie, hath executed true iudgement betweene man and man,
text.NABRE: 18:8 if he does not lend at interest or exact usury; if he refrains from evildoing and makes a fair judgment between two opponents;

text.NRSV: follows my statutes, and is careful to observe my ordinances, acting faithfully—such a one is righteous; he shall surely live, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: In my statutes, hath walked. And my regulations, hath observed to do them in truth, Righteous, he is, He shall surely live, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh.
text.KJV: 18:9 Hath walked in my Statutes, and hath kept my Iudgements to deale truely; hee is iust, hee shall surely liue, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 18:9 if he walks by my statutes and is careful to observe my ordinances, that man is just—he shall surely live—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: If he has a son who is violent, a shedder of blood,
text.Rotherham: But he hath begotten a son Who is a violent man A shedder of blood,- Who doeth the like of any of these things;
text.KJV: 18:10 ¶ If hee beget a sonne that is a robber, a shedder of blood, and that doth the like to any one of these things,
text.NABRE: 18:10 But if he begets a son who is violent and commits murder, or does any of these things,

text.NRSV: who does any of these things (though his father does none of them), who eats upon the mountains, defiles his neighbor’s wife,
text.Rotherham: Whereas he, none of those other things, doeth, For indeed upon the mountains, he hath eaten, And the wife of his neighbour, hath defiled;
text.KJV: 18:11 And that doeth not any of those duties, bnt euen hath eaten vpon the mountains, and defiled his neighbours wife,
text.NABRE: 18:11 even though the father does none of them—a son who eats on the mountains, defiles the wife of his neighbor,

text.NRSV: oppresses the poor and needy, commits robbery, does not restore the pledge, lifts up his eyes to the idols, commits abomination,
text.Rotherham: The oppressed and the needy, hath treated with violence, Hath ruthlessly plundered, The pledge, hath not restored, But unto the manufactured gods, hath lifted up his eyes, Abomination, hath wrought;
text.KJV: 18:12 Hath oppressed the poore and needie, hath spoiled by violence, hath not restored the pledge, and hath lift vp his eyes to the idoles, hath committed abomination,
text.NABRE: 18:12 oppresses the poor and needy, commits robbery, does not give back a pledge, raises his eyes to idols, does abominable things,

text.NRSV: lends at interest, and takes profit; shall he then live? He shall not. He has done all these abominable things; he shall surely die; his blood shall be upon himself.
text.Rotherham: Upon interest, hath put out. And increase, hath accepted—, And shall he live? He shall not live All these abominations, he hath done, He shall surely be put to death, His blood, upon himself, shall be.
text.KJV: 18:13 Hath giuen foorth vpon vsurie, and hath taken encrease: shall he then liue? hee shall not liue: hee hath done all these abominations, hee shall surely die, his blood shalbe vpon him.
text.NABRE: 18:13 lends at interest and exacts usury—this son certainly shall not live. Because he practiced all these abominations, he shall surely be put to death; his own blood shall be on him.

text.NRSV: But if this man has a son who sees all the sins that his father has done, considers, and does not do likewise,
text.Rotherham: But lo! he hath begotten a son, Who hath considered all the sins of his father which he hath done,- Yea he hath considered and not done like them:
text.KJV: 18:14 ¶ Now loe, if hee beget a sonne that seeth all his fathers sinnes which he hath done, and considereth, and doth not such like,
text.NABRE: 18:14 But, in turn, if he begets a son who sees all the sins his father commits, yet fears and does not imitate him—

text.NRSV: who does not eat upon the mountains or lift up his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, does not defile his neighbor’s wife,
text.Rotherham: Upon the mountains, hath not eaten, And his eyes, hath not lifted up unto the manufactured gods of the house of Israel, The wife of his neighbour, hath not defiled;
text.KJV: 18:15 That hath not eaten vpon the mountaines, neither hath lift vp his eyes to the idoles of the house of Israel, hath not defiled his neighbours wife,
text.NABRE: 18:15 a son who does not eat on the mountains, or raise his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel, or defile a neighbor’s wife;

text.NRSV: does not wrong anyone, exacts no pledge, commits no robbery, gives his bread to the hungry and covers the naked with a garment,
text.Rotherham: And no man, hath he treated with violence, Hath by no means withholden the pledge, And plunder, hath not seized,— His bread—to the famished, hath given And the naked, hath covered with clothing;
text.KJV: 18:16 Neither hath oppressed any, hath not withholden the pledge, neither hath spoiled by violence, but hath giuen his bread to the hungry, and hath couered the naked with a garment,
text.NABRE: 18:16 who does not oppress anyone, or exact a pledge, or commit robbery; who gives his food to the hungry and clothes the naked;

text.NRSV: withholds his hand from iniquity, takes no interest or profit, observes my ordinances, and follows my statutes; he shall not die for his father’s iniquity; he shall surely live.
text.Rotherham: From dishonesty, hath turned back his hand Neither interest nor increase, hath accepted, my regulations, hath executed, In my statutes, hath walked,— He shall not die for the iniquity of his father He shall surely live.
text.KJV: 18:17 That hath taken off his hand from the poore, that hath not receiued vsurie nor increase, hath executed my Iudgements, hath walked in my Statutes, he shall not die for the iniquitie of his father, he shall surely liue.
text.NABRE: 18:17 who refrains from evildoing, accepts no interest or usury, but keeps my ordinances and walks in my statutes—this one shall not die for the sins of his father. He shall surely live!

text.NRSV: As for his father, because he practiced extortion, robbed his brother, and did what is not good among his people, he dies for his iniquity.
text.Rotherham: His father Because he exacted unjust gain Seized plunder of a brother, And that which was not good, had done in the midst of his people Therefore lo! he died, in his iniquity.
text.KJV: 18:18 As for his father, because hee cruelly oppressed, spoiled his brother by violence, and did that which is not good among his people, loe, euen he shall die in his iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 18:18 Only the father, since he committed extortion and robbed his brother, and did what was not good among his people—he will die because of his sin!

text.NRSV: Yet you say, “Why should not the son suffer for the iniquity of the father?” When the son has done what is lawful and right, and has been careful to observe all my statutes, he shall surely live.
text.Rotherham: Will ye then say. Why hath not the son borne a part of the iniquity of the father? But the son hath done justice and righteousness, All my statutes, hath observed and done them He shall surely live.
text.KJV: 18:19 ¶ Yet say yee, Why? doeth not the sonne beare the iniquitie of the father? when the sonne hath done that which is lawfull and right, and hath kept all my Statutes, and hath done them, he shall surely liue.
text.NABRE: 18:19 You ask: “Why is not the son charged with the guilt of his father?” Because the son has done what is just and right and has been careful to observe all my statutes—he shall surely live!

text.NRSV: The person who sins shall die. A child shall not suffer for the iniquity of a parent, nor a parent suffer for the iniquity of a child; the righteousness of the righteous shall be his own, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be his own.
text.Rotherham: The person that sinneth, the same, shall die,— A, son, shall not bear a part of the iniquity of, the father Neither shall, a father, bear a part of the iniquity of the son, the righteousness of the righteous, upon himself, shall be. And, the lawlessness of a lawless man, upon himself, shall be.
text.KJV: 18:20 The soule that sinneth, it shal die: the sonne shall not beare the iniquitie of the father, neither shal the father beare the iniquitie of the sonne; the righteousnesse of the righteous shall bee vpon him, and the wickednesse of the wicked shalbe vpon him.
text.NABRE: 18:20 Only the one who sins shall die. The son shall not be charged with the guilt of his father, nor shall the father be charged with the guilt of his son. Justice belongs to the just, and wickedness to the wicked.

text.NRSV: But if the wicked turn away from all their sins that they have committed and keep all my statutes and do what is lawful and right, they shall surely live; they shall not die.
text.Rotherham: But as for the lawless man— When he shall turn back from all his sins which he hath committed, And observe all my statutes, And do justice and righteousness He shall surely live. He shall not die:
text.KJV: 18:21 But if the wicked will turne from all his sinnes that he hath committed and keepe all my statutes, and doe that which is lawful and right, he shall surely liue, he shall not die.
text.NABRE: 18:21 But if the wicked man turns away from all the sins he has committed, if he keeps all my statutes and does what is just and right, he shall surely live. He shall not die!

text.NRSV: None of the transgressions that they have committed shall be remembered against them; for the righteousness that they have done they shall live.
text.Rotherham: None of his transgressions which he hath committed shall be remembered against him, —In his righteousness which he hath done, he shall live.
text.KJV: 18:22 All his transgressions that he hath committed, they shall not be mentioned vnto him: in his righteousnesse, that he hath done, he shall liue.
text.NABRE: 18:22 None of the crimes he has committed shall be remembered against him; he shall live because of the justice he has shown.

text.NRSV: Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked, says the Lord God, and not rather that they should turn from their ways and live?
text.Rotherham: Could I take delight, in the death of the lawless? Demandeth My Lord. Yahweh. Must it not be in his turning from his ways in which case he shall live?
text.KJV: 18:23 Haue I any pleasure at all that the wicked should die, saith the Lord God ? And not that he should returne from his wayes, and liue?
text.NABRE: 18:23 Do I find pleasure in the death of the wicked—oracle of the Lord God? Do I not rejoice when they turn from their evil way and live?

text.NRSV: But when the righteous turn away from their righteousness and commit iniquity and do the same abominable things that the wicked do, shall they live? None of the righteous deeds that they have done shall be remembered; for the treachery of which they are guilty and the sin they have committed, they shall die.
text.Rotherham: But, when a righteous man, shall turn away, from his righteousness and do that which is perverse, Shall do, according to all the abominations which the lawless man hath done, Shall he live? None of his righteous acts which he hath done, shall be remembered, In his treachery wherein he hath been treacherous, And in his sin wherein he hath sinned, In them, shall he die.
text.KJV: 18:24 ¶ But when the righteous turneth away frō his righteousnes, & committeth iniquitie, and doth according to all the abominations that the wicked man doth, shall he liue? all his righteousnesse that he hath done, shall not be mentioned: in his trespasse that he hath trespassed, and in his sinne that he hath sinned, in them shall he die.
text.NABRE: 18:24 And if the just turn from justice and do evil, like all the abominations the wicked do, can they do this evil and still live? None of the justice they did shall be remembered, because they acted treacherously and committed these sins; because of this, they shall die.

text.NRSV: Yet you say, “The way of the Lord is unfair.” Hear now, O house of Israel: Is my way unfair? Is it not your ways that are unfair?
text.Rotherham: Can ye then say— The way of My Lord will not be equal? Hear I pray you O house of Israel, Will my way, not be equal? Will not your ways be unequal?
text.KJV: 18:25 ¶ Yet yee say; The way of the Lord is not equall. Heare now, O house of Israel; Is not my way equall? are not your wayes vnequall?
text.NABRE: 18:25 You say, “The Lord ’s way is not fair!” Hear now, house of Israel: Is it my way that is unfair? Are not your ways unfair?

text.NRSV: When the righteous turn away from their righteousness and commit iniquity, they shall die for it; for the iniquity that they have committed they shall die.
text.Rotherham: When a righteous man shall turn away from his righteousness and do that which is perverse and die because of those things In his own perversity which he hath done, shall he die.
text.KJV: 18:26 When a righteous man turneth away from his righteousnesse, & committeth iniquitie, and dieth in them; for his iniquitie that he hath done, shall he die.
text.NABRE: 18:26 When the just turn away from justice to do evil and die, on account of the evil they did they must die.

text.NRSV: Again, when the wicked turn away from the wickedness they have committed and do what is lawful and right, they shall save their life.
text.Rotherham: But when a lawless man turneth away from his lawlessness which he hath done, And hath done justice and righteousness He, shall save his own soul, alive:
text.KJV: 18:27 Againe, when the wicked man turneth away from his wickednesse that he hath committed, and doth that which is lawfull and right, he shall saue his soule aliue.
text.NABRE: 18:27 But if the wicked turn from the wickedness they did and do what is right and just, they save their lives;

text.NRSV: Because they considered and turned away from all the transgressions that they had committed, they shall surely live; they shall not die.
text.Rotherham: When he considered, then he turned away from all his transgressions which he had committed, He shall surely live. He shall not die.
text.KJV: 18:28 Because he considereth and turneth away from all his transgressions that he hath committed, he shall surely liue, he shall not die.
text.NABRE: 18:28 since they turned away from all the sins they committed, they shall live; they shall not die.

text.NRSV: Yet the house of Israel says, “The way of the Lord is unfair.” O house of Israel, are my ways unfair? Is it not your ways that are unfair?
text.Rotherham: Can the house of Israel then say- The way of My Lord will not be equal? Will my ways, not be equal O house of Israel? Will not your ways be unequal?
text.KJV: 18:29 Yet saith the house of Israel; The way of the Lord is not equall. O house of Israel, are not my wayes equall? are not your wayes vnequall?
text.NABRE: 18:29 But the house of Israel says, “The Lord’s way is not fair!” Is it my way that is not fair, house of Israel? Is it not your ways that are not fair?

text.NRSV: Therefore I will judge you, O house of Israel, all of you according to your ways, says the Lord God. Repent and turn from all your transgressions; otherwise iniquity will be your ruin.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Every man according to his own ways, will I judge you O house of Israel, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Return ye—and make good your return—from all your transgressions, That they become not unto you a stumbling block of iniquity.
text.KJV: 18:30 Therefore I will iudge you, O house of Israel, euery one according to his wayes, saith the Lord God; repent, and turne your selues from all your transgressions: so iniquitie shall not be your ruine.
text.NABRE: 18:30 Therefore I will judge you, house of Israel, all of you according to your ways—oracle of the Lord God. Turn, turn back from all your crimes, that they may not be a cause of sin for you ever again.

text.NRSV: Cast away from you all the transgressions that you have committed against me, and get yourselves a new heart and a new spirit! Why will you die, O house of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Cast off from you all your transgressions which ye have committed against me, And make you a new heart; And a new spirit,- For why should ye die, O house of Israel?
text.KJV: 18:31 ¶ Cast away from you all your transgressions, wherby yee haue transgressed, and make you a new heart and a new spirit: for why will yee die, O house of Israel?
text.NABRE: 18:31 Cast away from you all the crimes you have committed, and make for yourselves a new heart and a new spirit. Why should you die, house of Israel?

text.NRSV: For I have no pleasure in the death of anyone, says the Lord God. Turn, then, and live.
text.Rotherham: For I cannot take delight in the death of him that dieth, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Make good your return then and, live!
text.KJV: 18:32 For I haue no pleasure in þ death of him that dieth, saith the Lord God: wherefore turne your selues, & liue ye.
text.NABRE: 18:32 For I find no pleasure in the death of anyone who dies—oracle of the Lord God. Turn back and live!

text.NRSV: As for you, raise up a lamentation for the princes of Israel,
text.Rotherham: Thou. therefore take up a dirge, for the prince of Israel;
text.KJV: 19:1 Moreouer, take thou vp a lamentation for the princes of Israel,
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 Allegory of the Lions As for you, raise a lamentation over the princes of Israel,

text.NRSV: and say: What a lioness was your mother among lions! She lay down among young lions, rearing her whelps.
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say— How was thy mother a lioness, Between lions, she lay down,- In the midst of young lions, She nourished her whelps;
text.KJV: 19:2 And say, What is thy mother? a lyonesse: shee lay downe among lions, she nourished her whelpes among yong lions.
text.NABRE: 19:2 and say: What a lioness was your mother, a lion among lions! She made her lair among young lions, to raise her cubs;

text.NRSV: She brought up one of her whelps; he became a young lion, and he learned to catch prey; he devoured humans.
text.Rotherham: And she reared up one of her whelps— A young lion, he became, And he learned to rend prey. Men, he devoured.
text.KJV: 19:3 And shee brought vp one of her whelps: it became a yong lion, & it learned to catch the pray, it deuoured men.
text.NABRE: 19:3 One cub she raised up, a young lion he became; He learned to tear apart prey, he devoured people.

text.NRSV: The nations sounded an alarm against him; he was caught in their pit; and they brought him with hooks to the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then nations heard of him. In their pit, was he caught,— So they brought him with hooks. Into the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 19:4 The nations also heard of him, hee was taken in their pit, and they brought him with chaines vnto the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 19:4 Nations heard about him; in their pit he was caught; They took him away with hooks to the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: When she saw that she was thwarted, that her hope was lost, she took another of her whelps and made him a young lion.
text.Rotherham: And she saw she had waited Lost was her hope, Then took she another of her whelps. A young lion, she made him.
text.KJV: 19:5 Now when she saw that shee had waited, and her hope was lost, then she tooke another of her whelps, and made him a yong lion.
text.NABRE: 19:5 When she realized she had waited in vain, she lost hope. She took another of her cubs, and made him a young lion.

text.NRSV: He prowled among the lions; he became a young lion, and he learned to catch prey; he devoured humans.
text.Rotherham: Yea he went to and fro amidst lions A young lion, he became,- And he learned to rend prey. Men, he devoured;
text.KJV: 19:6 And he went vp and downe among the lions, he became a yong lion, and learned to catch the pray, and deuoured men.
text.NABRE: 19:6 He prowled among the lions, became a young lion; He learned to tear apart prey, he devoured people.

text.NRSV: He ravaged their strongholds, and laid waste their cities; the land was appalled, and all in it, at the sound of his roaring.
text.Rotherham: And he injured his widows, And their cities, laid waste,- And deserted was the land and its fulness, At the noise of his roaring.
text.KJV: 19:7 And he knew there desolate palaces, and he laied waste their cities, and the land was desolate, and the fulnesse thereof by the noise of his roaring.
text.NABRE: 19:7 He ravaged their strongholds, laid waste their cities. The earth and everything in it were terrified at the sound of his roar.

text.NRSV: The nations set upon him from the provinces all around; they spread their net over him; he was caught in their pit.
text.Rotherham: Then set upon him the nations round about. From the provinces,- And spread over him their net In their pit, was he caught;
text.KJV: 19:8 Then the nations set against him on euery side from the prouinces, and spread their net ouer him: he was taken in their pit.
text.NABRE: 19:8 Nations laid out against him snares all around; They spread their net for him, in their pit he was caught.

text.NRSV: With hooks they put him in a cage, and brought him to the king of Babylon; they brought him into custody, so that his voice should no longer be heard on the mountains of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And they put him in a cage with hooks, And brought him to Babylon’s king,— They brought him into strong-holds, That his noise might be heard no more Among the mountains of Israel.
text.KJV: 19:9 And they put him in ward in chaines, and brought him to the king of Babylon, they brought him into holds, that his voyce should no more be heard vpon the mountaines of Israel.
text.NABRE: 19:9 They put him in fetters and took him away to the king of Babylon, So his roar would no longer be heard on the mountains of Israel.

text.NRSV: Your mother was like a vine in a vineyard transplanted by the water, fruitful and full of branches from abundant water.
text.Rotherham: Thy mother [was] like a vine of thy vineyard, By waters, was planted,— Fruitful and full of branches, she became By reason of waters abundant;
text.KJV: 19:10 ¶ Thy mother is like a vine in thy blood, planted by the waters, she was fruitfull and full of branches by reason of many waters,
text.NABRE: 19:10 Allegory of the Vine Branch Your mother was like a leafy vine planted by water, Fruitful and full of branches because of abundant water.

text.NRSV: Its strongest stem became a ruler’s scepter; it towered aloft among the thick boughs; it stood out in its height with its mass of branches.
text.Rotherham: And they served her as staves of power For the sceptres of rulers, And high became the stature thereof With its interwoven foliage,- And it was seen by its height, By its multitude of branches.
text.KJV: 19:11 And she had strong rods for the scepters of them that beare rule, and her stature was exalted among the thicke branches, & she appeared in her height with the multitude of her branches.
text.NABRE: 19:11 One strong branch grew into a royal scepter. So tall it towered among the clouds, conspicuous in height, with dense foliage.

text.NRSV: But it was plucked up in fury, cast down to the ground; the east wind dried it up; its fruit was stripped off, its strong stem was withered; the fire consumed it.
text.Rotherham: Then was she uprooted in indignation To the ground, was she cast, And an east wind, dried up her fruit,—Broken off and withered were her staves of power, A fire, devoured them.
text.KJV: 19:12 But she was plucked vp in fury: she was cast downe to the ground, and the East wind dryed vp her fruite: her strong rods were broken and withered, the fire consumed them.
text.NABRE: 19:12 But she was torn out in fury and flung to the ground; The east wind withered her up, her fruit was plucked away; Her strongest branch dried up, fire devoured it.

text.NRSV: Now it is transplanted into the wilderness, into a dry and thirsty land.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, is she planted in a desert, -In a land parched and dry;
text.KJV: 19:13 And now she is planted in the wildernesse, in a dry and thirsty ground.
text.NABRE: 19:13 Now she is planted in a wilderness, in a dry, parched land.

text.NRSV: And fire has gone out from its stem, has consumed its branches and fruit, so that there remains in it no strong stem, no scepter for a ruler. This is a lamentation, and it is used as a lamentation.
text.Rotherham: And there hath gone forth a fire out of her staves of rods. Her fruit, hath it devoured, And there is in her no staff of power. As a sceptre to bear rule. A dirge, it is, and hath been made a dirge.
text.KJV: 19:14 And fire is gone out of a rod of her branches, which hath deuoured her fruite, so that she hath no strong rod to be a scepter to rule: this is a lamentation, and shall be for a lamentation.
text.NABRE: 19:14 Fire flashed from her branch, and devoured her shoots; Now she does not have a strong branch, a royal scepter! This is a lamentation and serves as a lamentation.

text.NRSV: In the seventh year, in the fifth month, on the tenth day of the month, certain elders of Israel came to consult the Lord, and sat down before me.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the seventh year in the fifth month, on the tent of the month, that there came in men of the elders of Israel to enquire of Yahweh,—and they sat before me.
text.KJV: 20:1 And it came to passe in the seuenth yeere, in the fift moneth, the tenth day of the moneth, that certaine of the elders of Israel came to enquire of the Lord, and sate before me.
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 Israel’s History of Infidelity. In the seventh year, on the tenth day of the fifth month, some of the elders of Israel came to consult the Lord and sat down before me.

text.NRSV: And the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto me saying:-
text.KJV: 20:2 Then came the word of the Lord vnto me, saying,
text.NABRE: 20:2 Then the word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, speak to the elders of Israel and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: Why are you coming? To consult me? As I live, says the Lord God, I will not be consulted by you.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Speak thou with the elders of Israel and say unto them, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, To enquire of me, are ye coming in? As I live, verily I will not be enquired of by you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 20:3 Sonne of man, speake vnto the elders of Israel, and say vnto them, Thus saith the Lord God, Are yee come to enquire of me? As I liue, saith the Lord God, I will not be enquired of by you.
text.NABRE: 20:3 Son of man, speak to the elders of Israel and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: Have you come to consult me? As I live, I will not allow myself to be consulted by you!—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: Will you judge them, mortal, will you judge them? Then let them know the abominations of their ancestors,
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou judge them, wilt thou judge, O son of man? The abominations of their fathers, let them know:
text.KJV: 20:4 Wilt thou iudge them, sonne of man, wilt thou iudge them? cause them to know the abominations of their fathers:
text.NABRE: 20:4 Will you judge them? Will you judge, son of man? Tell them about the abominations of their ancestors,

text.NRSV: and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: On the day when I chose Israel, I swore to the offspring of the house of Jacob—making myself known to them in the land of Egypt—I swore to them, saying, I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt say unto them Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, In the day when I made choice of Israel, then lifted I up my hand. to the seed of the house of Jacob, and made myself known to them, in the land of Egypt,- Yea I lifted up my hand to them, saying, I Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 20:5 ¶ And say vnto them, Thus saith the Lord God, In the day when I chose Israel, and lifted vp mine hand vnto the seed of the house of Iacob, and made my selfe knowen vnto them in the land of Egypt, when I lifted vp mine hand vnto them, saying, I am the Lord your God,
text.NABRE: 20:5 and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: The day I chose Israel, I swore to the descendants of the house of Jacob; I revealed myself to them in the land of Egypt and swore to them, saying: I am the Lord, your God.

text.NRSV: On that day I swore to them that I would bring them out of the land of Egypt into a land that I had searched out for them, a land flowing with milk and honey, the most glorious of all lands.
text.Rotherham: In that day, lifted I up my hand to them, to bring them forth out of the land of Egypt,—into a land that I had looked out for them flowing with milk and honey, The beauty, it was of all lands,
text.KJV: 20:6 In the day that I lifted vp mine hand vnto them to bring them foorth of the land of Egypt, into a lande that I had espied for them, flowing with milke and hony, which is the glory of all lands:
text.NABRE: 20:6 That day I swore to bring them out of the land of Egypt to the land I had searched out for them, a land flowing with milk and honey, a jewel among all lands.

text.NRSV: And I said to them, Cast away the detestable things your eyes feast on, every one of you, and do not defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt; I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And I said unto them. Every one the abomination of his eyes, cast ye away, And with the manufactured gods of Egypt, let it not be that ye defile yourselves: I, Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 20:7 Then said I vnto them, Cast ye away euery man the abominations of his eyes, and defile not your selues with the idoles of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 20:7 Then I said to them: Throw away, each of you, the detestable things that held your eyes; do not defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt: I am the Lord, your God.

text.NRSV: But they rebelled against me and would not listen to me; not one of them cast away the detestable things their eyes feasted on, nor did they forsake the idols of Egypt. Then I thought I would pour out my fury upon them and spend my anger against them in the midst of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: But they rebelled against me And were not willing to hearken unto me, Every man the abominations of their eyes, they cast not away, And the manufactured gods of Egypt, forsook they not; Therefore spake I of pouring out mine indignation upon them to bring mine anger to an end against them, in the midst of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 20:8 But they rebelled against me, and would not hearken vnto mee: they did not euery man cast away the abominations of their eyes, neither did they forsake the idoles of Egypt: then I said, I will powre out my furie vpon them, to accomplish my anger against them in the middest of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 20:8 But they rebelled and refused to listen to me; none of them threw away the detestable things that held their eyes, nor did they abandon the idols of Egypt. Then I considered pouring out my fury and spending my anger against them there in the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: But I acted for the sake of my name, that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations among whom they lived, in whose sight I made myself known to them in bringing them out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I wrought with effect, for the sake of mine own Name, that it might not be profaned—before the eyes of the nations in whose midst they were,—before whose eyes I made myself known unto them, by bringing them forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 20:9 But I wrought for my names sake that it should not be polluted before the heathen, among whom they were, in whose sight I made my selfe knowen vnto them, in bringing them foorth out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 20:9 I acted for the sake of my name, that it should not be desecrated in the eyes of the nations among whom they were: in the eyes of the nations I had made myself known to them, to bring them out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: So I led them out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Therefore brought I them forth out of the land of Egypt,—and caused them to come into the desert.
text.KJV: 20:10 ¶ Wherefore I caused them to goe foorth out of the land of Egypt, and brought them into the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: 20:10 Therefore I led them out of the land of Egypt and brought them into the wilderness.

text.NRSV: I gave them my statutes and showed them my ordinances, by whose observance everyone shall live.
text.Rotherham: Then gave I unto them my statutes, And my regulations, caused I them to know,- By the which the man that doeth them shall live.
text.KJV: 20:11 And I gaue them my statutes, and shewed them my iudgements, which if a man doe, he shall euen liue in them.
text.NABRE: 20:11 Then I gave them my statutes and made known to them my ordinances, so that everyone who keeps them has life through them.

text.NRSV: Moreover I gave them my sabbaths, as a sign between me and them, so that they might know that I the Lord sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also my sabbaths, gave I unto them, to become a sign betwixt me and them,—that it might be known that, I, Yahweh, was hallowing them.
text.KJV: 20:12 Moreouer also, I gaue them my Sabbaths, to be a signe betweene mee and them, that they might know that I am the Lord that sanctifie them.
text.NABRE: 20:12 I also gave them my sabbaths to be a sign between me and them, to show that it is I, the Lord, who makes them holy.

text.NRSV: But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness; they did not observe my statutes but rejected my ordinances, by whose observance everyone shall live; and my sabbaths they greatly profaned. Then I thought I would pour out my fury upon them in the wilderness, to make a full end of them.
text.Rotherham: But the house of Israel, rebelled against me, in the desert, In my statutes, walked they not, And my regulations, they rejected By the which the man, that doeth them, shall live, My sabbaths also, they profaned exceedingly. Therefore spake I of pouring out mine indignation upon them in the desert, to make an end of them.
text.KJV: 20:13 But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wildernesse: they walked not in my statutes, and they despised my iudgements, which if a man doe, hee shall euen liue in them, and my sabbaths they greatly polluted: then I said I would powre out my furie vpon them in the wildernesse, to consume them:
text.NABRE: 20:13 But the house of Israel rebelled against me in the wilderness. They did not observe my statutes, and they rejected my ordinances that bring life to those who keep them. My sabbaths, too, they desecrated grievously. Then I considered pouring out my fury on them in the wilderness to put an end to them,

text.NRSV: But I acted for the sake of my name, so that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations, in whose sight I had brought them out.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I wrought with effect for the sake of mine own Name,—that it should not be profaned in the eyes of the nations, in whose eyes I had brought them forth.
text.KJV: 20:14 But I wrought for my names sake, that it should not bee polluted before the heathen, in whose sight I brought them out.
text.NABRE: 20:14 but I acted for the sake of my name, so it would not be desecrated in the eyes of the nations in whose sight I had brought them out.

text.NRSV: Moreover I swore to them in the wilderness that I would not bring them into the land that I had given them, a land flowing with milk and honey, the most glorious of all lands,
text.Rotherham: Yet even I lifted up my hand to them. in the desert,— That I would not bring them into the land which I had given to them flowing with milk and honey, The beauty, it was. of all lands:
text.KJV: 20:15 Yet also I lifted vp my hand vnto them in the wildernesse, that I would not bring them into the land which I had giuen them, flowing with milke and hony, which is the glory of all lands,
text.NABRE: 20:15 Nevertheless in the wilderness I swore to them that I would not bring them into the land I had given them—a land flowing with milk and honey, a jewel among all the lands.

text.NRSV: because they rejected my ordinances and did not observe my statutes, and profaned my sabbaths; for their heart went after their idols.
text.Rotherham: Because my regulations, they had rejected. And as for my statutes, they had not walked therein, My sabbaths also, had they profaned; For after their manufactured gods, their heart had been going.
text.KJV: 20:16 Because they despised my iudgements, and walked not in my statutes, but polluted my Sabbaths: for their heart went after their idoles.
text.NABRE: 20:16 Their hearts followed after their idols so closely that they did not live by my statutes, but rejected my ordinances and desecrated my sabbaths.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless my eye spared them, and I did not destroy them or make a full end of them in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless mine eye threw a shield over them that they should not be destroyed; So I made not of them a full end in the desert.
text.KJV: 20:17 Neuerthelesse, mine eye spared them from destroying them, neither did I make an end of thē in the wildernes.
text.NABRE: 20:17 But I looked on them with pity, not wanting to destroy them, so I did not put an end to them in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: I said to their children in the wilderness, Do not follow the statutes of your parents, nor observe their ordinances, nor defile yourselves with their idols.
text.Rotherham: But I said unto their children in the desert, In the statutes of your fathers, let it not be that ye walk, And their regulations, let it not be that ye observe,— And with their manufactured gods, let it not be that ye defile yourselves.
text.KJV: 20:18 But I said vnto their children in the wildernesse; Walke ye not in the statutes of your fathers, neither obserue their iudgements, nor defile your selues with their idoles.
text.NABRE: 20:18 Then I said to their children in the wilderness: Do not follow the statutes of your parents. Do not keep their ordinances. Do not defile yourselves with their idols.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God; follow my statutes, and be careful to observe my ordinances,
text.Rotherham: I, Yahweh, am your God, In my statutes, walk ye,— And my regulations, observe ye and do them;
text.KJV: 20:19 I am the Lord your God: walke in my statutes, and keepe my iudgements and doe them:
text.NABRE: 20:19 I am the Lord, your God: follow my statutes and be careful to observe my ordinances;

text.NRSV: and hallow my sabbaths that they may be a sign between me and you, so that you may know that I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: My sabbaths also, hallow ye,— And they shall become a sign betwixt me and you, That it may be known that I Yahweh am your God.
text.KJV: 20:20 And hallow my Sabbaths, and they shall be a signe betweene mee and you, that yee may know that I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 20:20 keep holy my sabbaths as a sign between me and you so that you may know that I am the Lord, your God.

text.NRSV: But the children rebelled against me; they did not follow my statutes, and were not careful to observe my ordinances, by whose observance everyone shall live; they profaned my sabbaths. Then I thought I would pour out my fury upon them and spend my anger against them in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding the children rebelled against me, In my statutes, walked they not, And my regulations, observed they not to do them Wherein the man who shall do them shall live, My sabbaths, they profaned. Therefore spake I of pouring out mine indignation upon them to bring mine anger to an end against them. in the desert.
text.KJV: 20:21 Notwithstanding the children rebelled against me: they walked not in my statutes, neither kept my iudgements to doe them, which if a man doe, hee shall euen liue in them; they polluted my Sabbaths: then I said I would powre out my furie vpon them, to accomplish my anger against them in the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: 20:21 But their children rebelled against me: they did not follow my statutes or keep my ordinances that bring life to those who observe them; my sabbaths they desecrated. Then I considered pouring out my fury on them, spending my anger against them in the wilderness;

text.NRSV: But I withheld my hand, and acted for the sake of my name, so that it should not be profaned in the sight of the nations, in whose sight I had brought them out.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I withdrew my hand, and wrought with effect for the sake of mine own Name, —that it should not be profaned before the eyes of the nations, before whose eyes I had brought them forth:
text.KJV: 20:22 Neuerthelesse I withdrew mine hand and wrought for my names sake, that it should not be polluted in the fight of the heathen, in whose sight I brought them foorth.
text.NABRE: 20:22 but I stayed my hand, acting for the sake of my name, lest it be desecrated in the eyes of the nations, in whose sight I had brought them out.

text.NRSV: Moreover I swore to them in the wilderness that I would scatter them among the nations and disperse them through the countries,
text.Rotherham: Even, I, lifted up my hand to them in the desert,— That I would disperse them throughout the nations, And scatter them throughout the lands;
text.KJV: 20:23 I lifted vp mine hand vnto them also in the wildernesse, that I would scatter them among the heathen, and disperse them through the countreys;
text.NABRE: 20:23 Nevertheless I swore to them in the wilderness that I would disperse them among the nations and scatter them in other lands,

text.NRSV: because they had not executed my ordinances, but had rejected my statutes and profaned my sabbaths, and their eyes were set on their ancestors’ idols.
text.Rotherham: Because my regulations, had they not done And my statutes, they had rejected, My sabbaths also, they had profaned,- And after the manufactured gods of their fathers, were their eyes turned.
text.KJV: 20:24 Because they had not executed my iudgements, but had despised my Statutes, and had polluted my Sabbaths, and their eyes were after their fathers idoles.
text.NABRE: 20:24 because they did not carry out my ordinances, but rejected my statutes and desecrated my sabbaths, having eyes only for the idols of their ancestors.

text.NRSV: Moreover I gave them statutes that were not good and ordinances by which they could not live.
text.Rotherham: I myself, indeed, suffered them to walk in statutes which were not good,— And regulations in which they could not live.
text.KJV: 20:25 Wherefore I gaue them also statutes that were not good, and iudgements whereby they should not liue.
text.NABRE: 20:25 Therefore I gave them statutes that were not good, and ordinances through which they could not have life.

text.NRSV: I defiled them through their very gifts, in their offering up all their firstborn, in order that I might horrify them, so that they might know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yea I let them defile themselves with their gifts, when they caused every firstborn to pass through the fire,-.that I might make them desolate. To the end they might know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 20:26 And I polluted them in their owne gifts, in that they caused to passe through the fire all that openeth the wombe, that I might make them desolate, to the end, that they might know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 20:26 I let them become defiled by their offerings, by having them make a fiery offering of every womb’s firstborn, in order to ruin them so they might know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore, mortal, speak to the house of Israel and say to them, Thus says the Lord God: In this again your ancestors blasphemed me, by dealing treacherously with me.
text.Rotherham: Therefore speak thou unto the house of Israel Son of man, and say unto them, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh,— Yet further in this, did your fathers insult me, by the treachery wherewith they dealt treacherously against me;
text.KJV: 20:27 ¶ Therefore sonne of man, speake vnto the house of Israel, and say vnto them, Thus saith the Lord God, Yet in this your fathers haue blasphemed me, in that they haue committed a trespasse against me.
text.NABRE: 20:27 Therefore speak to the house of Israel, son of man, and tell them: Thus says the Lord God: In this way also your ancestors blasphemed me, breaking faith with me.

text.NRSV: For when I had brought them into the land that I swore to give them, then wherever they saw any high hill or any leafy tree, there they offered their sacrifices and presented the provocation of their offering; there they sent up their pleasing odors, and there they poured out their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: When I brought them into the land as to which I had lifted up mine hand to give it them, then beheld they every high hill and every tangled tree And offered there their sacrifices, And presented there, their provoking gift, And placed there, their satisfying odour, And poured out there, their drink- offerings.
text.KJV: 20:28 For when I had brought them into the land, for the which I lifted vp mine hand to giue it to them, then they saw euery high hill, and all the thicke trees, and they offered there their sacrifices, and there they presented the prouocation of their offering: there also they made their sweet sauour, and powred out there their drinke offerings.
text.NABRE: 20:28 When I brought them to the land I had sworn to give them, and they saw all its high hills and leafy trees, there they offered sacrifices, there they made offerings to provoke me, there they sent up sweet-smelling oblations, there they poured out their libations.

text.NRSV: I said to them, What is the high place to which you go? So it is called Bamah to this day.
text.Rotherham: Therefore said I unto them, What! a high-place, whereinto ye are entering? And so the name thereof hath been called High-place, until this day.
text.KJV: 20:29 Then I said vnto them, What is the high place whereunto ye goe? and the name thereof is called Bamah vnto this day.
text.NABRE: 20:29 So I said to them, “What is this high place to which you go?” Thus its name became “high place” even to this day.

text.NRSV: Therefore say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God: Will you defile yourselves after the manner of your ancestors and whore after their detestable things?
text.Rotherham: Therefore say thou unto the house of Israel. Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Is it in the way of your fathers, ye are defiling yourselves,- And after their abominations, that ye are committing lewdness?
text.KJV: 20:30 Wherefore say vnto the house of Israel, Thus saith the Lord God, Are ye polluted after the maner of your fathers? and commit ye whoredome after their abominations?
text.NABRE: 20:30 Therefore say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord God: Will you defile yourselves in the way your ancestors did? Will you lust after their detestable idols?

text.NRSV: When you offer your gifts and make your children pass through the fire, you defile yourselves with all your idols to this day. And shall I be consulted by you, O house of Israel? As I live, says the Lord God, I will not be consulted by you.
text.Rotherham: Yea when ye lift up your gifts When ye cause your children to pass through the fire, Ye are defiling yourselves unto all your manufactured gods unto this day, And shall, I, be enquired of by you O house of Israel? As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Surely I will not be enquired of by you.
text.KJV: 20:31 For when yee offer your gifts, when yee make your sonnes to passe through the fire, ye pollute your selues with all your idoles euen vnto this day: and shall I be inquired of by you, O house of Israel? As I liue, saith the Lord God, I will not be inquired of by you.
text.NABRE: 20:31 By offering your gifts, by making your children pass through the fire, you defile yourselves with all your idols even to this day. Shall I let myself be consulted by you, house of Israel? As I live—oracle of the Lord God —I swear I will not let myself be consulted by you!

text.NRSV: What is in your mind shall never happen—the thought, “Let us be like the nations, like the tribes of the countries, and worship wood and stone.”
text.Rotherham: And as for that which is rising up on your spirit, it shall, not at all come to pass, In that ye are saying Let us be like the nations Like the families of the lands, By ministering unto Wood and Stone!
text.KJV: 20:32 And that which cōmeth into your minde, shall not be at all, that ye say, We wil be as the heathen, as the families of the countreys, to serue wood and stone.
text.NABRE: 20:32 What has entered your mind shall never happen: You are thinking, “We shall be like the nations, like the peoples of foreign lands, serving wood and stone.”

text.NRSV: As I live, says the Lord God, surely with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out, I will be king over you.
text.Rotherham: As I live, Declareth My Lord Yahweh, Surely, with a strong hand and With an outstretched arm and With outpoured indignation, Will I be king over you;
text.KJV: 20:33 ¶ As I liue, sayeth the Lord God, surely with a mighty hand, and with a stretched out arme, and with furie powred out, will I rule ouer you.
text.NABRE: 20:33 As I live—oracle of the Lord God —with mighty hand and outstretched arm, with wrath poured out, I swear I will be king over you!

text.NRSV: I will bring you out from the peoples and gather you out of the countries where you are scattered, with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, and with wrath poured out;
text.Rotherham: And I will bring you forth from among the peoples, And gather you together out of the lands, throughout which ye have been dispersed, With a strong hand and With an outstretched arm, and With outpoured indignation;
text.KJV: 20:34 And I will bring you out from the people, and will gather you out of the countreys wherein ye are scattered, with a mighty hand, & with a stretched out arme, and with fury powred out.
text.NABRE: 20:34 With mighty hand and outstretched arm, with wrath poured out, I will bring you out from the nations and gather you from the countries over which you are scattered;

text.NRSV: and I will bring you into the wilderness of the peoples, and there I will enter into judgment with you face to face.
text.Rotherham: And I will bring you into the desert of the peoples,—and will contend with you there, face to face.
text.KJV: 20:35 And I wil bring you into the wildernes of the people, and there will I plead with you face to face.
text.NABRE: 20:35 I will lead you to the wilderness of the peoples and enter into judgment with you face to face.

text.NRSV: As I entered into judgment with your ancestors in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so I will enter into judgment with you, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Just as I contended with your fathers in the desert of the land of Egypt, So will I contend with you, Declareth My Lord Yahweh;
text.KJV: 20:36 Like as I pleaded with your fathers in the wildernes of the land of Egypt, so wil I plead with you, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 20:36 Just as I entered into judgment with your ancestors in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so will I enter into judgment with you—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: I will make you pass under the staff, and will bring you within the bond of the covenant.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause you to pass under the rod And will bring you into the bond of the covenant;
text.KJV: 20:37 And I will cause you to passe vnder the rod, and I will bring you into the bond of the Couenant.
text.NABRE: 20:37 Thus I will make you pass under the staff and will impose on you the terms of the covenant.

text.NRSV: I will purge out the rebels among you, and those who transgress against me; I will bring them out of the land where they reside as aliens, but they shall not enter the land of Israel. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And will sever from you those who are rebelling and those who are transgressing against me, Out of the land of their sojournings, will I bring them forth, Yet upon the soil of Israel, shall they not enter, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 20:38 And I will purge out from among you the rebels, and them that transgresse against mee: I will bring them foorth out of the countrey where they soiourne, and they shall not enter into the land of Israel, and yee shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 20:38 I will sort out from you those who defied me and rebelled against me; from the land where they resided as aliens I will bring them out, but they shall not return to the land of Israel. Thus you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: As for you, O house of Israel, thus says the Lord God: Go serve your idols, every one of you now and hereafter, if you will not listen to me; but my holy name you shall no more profane with your gifts and your idols.
