text.KJV: 24:3 And vtter a parable vnto the rebellious house, and say vnto them, Thus sayth the Lord God, Set on a pot, set it on, and also powre water into it.
text.NABRE: 24:3 Propose this parable to the rebellious house and say to them: Thus says the Lord God: Put the pot on, put it on! Pour in some water;

text.NRSV: put in it the pieces, all the good pieces, the thigh and the shoulder; fill it with choice bones.
text.Rotherham: Gather the pieces thereof into it, Every good piece thigh and shoulder, With the choice of the bones, fill it:
text.KJV: 24:4 Gather the pieces thereof into it, euen euery good piece, the thigh, and the shoulder; fill it with the choice bones.
text.NABRE: 24:4 Add to it pieces of meat, all choice pieces; With thigh and shoulder, with choice cuts fill it.

text.NRSV: Take the choicest of the flock, pile the logs under it; boil its pieces, seethe also its bones in it.
text.Rotherham: Take of the choice of the rock, Moreover also pile of bones place under it,- Boil the pieces thereof, Yea let the bones of it seethe in the midst thereof.
text.KJV: 24:5 Take the choice of the flocke, and burne also the bones vnder it, and make it boyle well, and let him seethe the bones of it therein.
text.NABRE: 24:5 Choose the pick of the flock, then pile logs beneath it; Bring it to a boil, cook all the pieces in it.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city, to the pot whose rust is in it, whose rust has not gone out of it! Take out of it piece after piece; no lot has fallen on it.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Woe! city of bloodshed. The caldron whose scum is in it, and whose scum, hath not gone out of it: Piece by piece, bring it out, There hath fallen thereon no lot.
text.KJV: 24:6 ¶ Wherefore thus sayth the Lord God, Woe to the bloodie citie, to the pot whose scumme is therein, and whose scumme is not gone out of it; bring it out piece by piece, let no lot fall vpon it.
text.NABRE: 24:6 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the city full of blood! A pot containing filth, whose filth cannot be removed! Take out its pieces one by one, for no lot has fallen on their behalf.

text.NRSV: For the blood she shed is inside it; she placed it on the bare rock; she did not pour it out on the ground, to cover it with earth.
text.Rotherham: For, her own blood, hath come to be in her midst, Upon the smooth face of the cliff, hath she set it,- She hath not poured it out on the earth, that she might cover it with dust.
text.KJV: 24:7 For her blood is in the middest of her: she set it vpon the toppe of a rocke, she powred it vpon the ground to couer it with dust:
text.NABRE: 24:7 For her blood is still in her midst; on a bare rock she left it; She did not pour it on the ground to be covered with dirt.

text.NRSV: To rouse my wrath, to take vengeance, I have placed the blood she shed on the bare rock, so that it may not be covered.
text.Rotherham: To bring up indignation to execute an avenging, have I set her blood upon the smooth face of the cliff,—that it may not be covered.
text.KJV: 24:8 That it might cause furie to come vp to take vengeance: I haue set her blood vpon the top of a rocke, that it should not be couered.
text.NABRE: 24:8 To arouse wrath, to exact vengeance, I have left her blood on bare rock not to be covered.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Woe to the bloody city! I will even make the pile great.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Woe! city of bloodshed,—Even I myself, will make large the pile.
text.KJV: 24:9 Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Woe to the bloody citie, I will euen make the pile for fire, great.
text.NABRE: 24:9 Therefore, thus says the Lord God: Woe to the city full of blood! I will make the pyre great!

text.NRSV: Heap up the logs, kindle the fire; boil the meat well, mix in the spices, and let the bones be burned.
text.Rotherham: Heap on the wood Kindle the fire, Consume the flesh, Yea season with spice, And let the bones be scorched.
text.KJV: 24:10 Heape on wood, kindle the fire, consume the flesh, and spice it well, and let the bones be burnt.
text.NABRE: 24:10 Pile on the wood, kindle the fire. Cook the meat, stir the spicy mixture, char the bones!

text.NRSV: Then set it empty upon the coals, so that it may become hot, and its copper glow, so that its filth may be melted in it, its rust consumed.
text.Rotherham: Then set it upon the live coals thereof empty,—that the copper of it may be scorched and burn. So that the impurity thereof may be annihilated within it The scum thereof be consumed,
text.KJV: 24:11 Then set it empty vpon the coales thereof, that the brasse of it may be hot and may burne, and that the filthinesse of it may be molten in it, that the scum of it may be consumed.
text.NABRE: 24:11 Then set it empty on the coals, to heat up until its copper glows, So its impurities melt, its filth disappears.

text.NRSV: In vain I have wearied myself; its thick rust does not depart. To the fire with its rust!
text.Rotherham: With toils, hath she wearied herself,—Since her abundant scum will not go out of her, into the fire, with her scum!
text.KJV: 24:12 She hath wearied herselfe with lies, and her great scumme went not forth out of her: her scumme shall be in the fire.
text.NABRE: 24:12 The toil is exhausting, but the great filth will not come out— Filth, even with fire.

text.NRSV: Because of your filthy lewdness, because I would have cleansed you and you were not cleansed from your filthiness, you shall not be cleansed any more until I have satisfied my fury upon you.
text.Rotherham: In thine uncleanness, is lewdness; be- cause I purified thee and thou wast not purified, from thine uncleanness, thou shalt not be purified any more, until I have let mine indignation rest upon thee.
text.KJV: 24:13 In thy filthinesse is lewdnesse, because I haue purged thee, and thou wast not purged, thou shalt not be purged from thy filthinesse any more, till I haue caused my fury to rest vpon thee.
text.NABRE: 24:13 Even in defiling yourself with depravity I would still have cleansed you, but you would not have your impurity cleansed. You will not be cleansed now until I wreak my fury on you.

text.NRSV: I the Lord have spoken; the time is coming, I will act. I will not refrain, I will not spare, I will not relent. According to your ways and your deeds you shall be judged, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: I Yahweh, have spoken It cometh and I will perform I will not let alone, neither will I shield, Nor will I repent,- According to thy ways and according to thy doings, have they judged thee, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 24:14 I the Lord haue spoken it, it shall come to passe, and I will doe it, I will not goe backe, neither will I spare, neither will I repent, according to thy wayes and according to thy doings, shall they iudge thee, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 24:14 I, the Lord, have spoken; it will happen! I will do it and not hold back! I will not have pity or relent. By your conduct and deeds you shall be judged— oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: 24:15 ¶ Also the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: 24:15 Ezekiel as a Sign for the Exiles. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, with one blow I am about to take away from you the delight of your eyes; yet you shall not mourn or weep, nor shall your tears run down.
text.Rotherham: Son of Man Behold me! taking away from thee the delight of thine eyes, with a stroke,- But thou shall not lament Neither shalt thou weep, Neither shall come—thy tears:
text.KJV: 24:16 Sonne of man, behold, I take away from thee the desire of thine eyes with a stroke: yet neither shalt thou mourne, nor weepe, neither shall thy teares runne downe.
text.NABRE: 24:16 Son of man, with a sudden blow I am taking away from you the delight of your eyes, but do not mourn or weep or shed any tears.

text.NRSV: Sigh, but not aloud; make no mourning for the dead. Bind on your turban, and put your sandals on your feet; do not cover your upper lip or eat the bread of mourners.
text.Rotherham: To groan, forbear, Over the dead—no mourning, shalt thou make Thy chaplet, bind thou on thee And thy sandals:, put thou on thy feet, And thou shalt not cover thy beard, And the bread of other men, shalt thou not eat.
text.KJV: 24:17 Forbeare to crie, make no mourning for the dead, bind the tire of thine head vpon thee, and put on thy shooes vpon thy feete, and couer not thy lips, and eate not the bread of men.
text.NABRE: 24:17 Groan, moan for the dead, but make no public lament; bind on your turban, put your sandals on your feet, but do not cover your beard or eat the bread of mourners.

text.NRSV: So I spoke to the people in the morning, and at evening my wife died. And on the next morning I did as I was commanded.
text.Rotherham: So I spake unto the people in the morning, and my wife died in the evening,—and I did in the morning, as I had been commanded.
text.KJV: 24:18 So I spake vnto the people in the morning, and at euen my wife died, and I did in the morning as I was commanded.
text.NABRE: 24:18 I spoke to the people in the morning. In the evening my wife died. The next morning I did as I had been commanded.

text.NRSV: Then the people said to me, “Will you not tell us what these things mean for us, that you are acting this way?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the people unto me: Wilt thou not tell us what these things are to us, that thou shouldst be acting thus?
text.KJV: 24:19 ¶ And the people said vnto me; Wilt thou not tell vs what these things are to vs, that thou doest so?
text.NABRE: 24:19 Then the people asked me, “Will you not tell us what all these things you are doing mean for us?”

text.NRSV: Then I said to them: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: So I said unto them: The word of Yahweh hath come unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 24:20 Then I answered them, The word of the Lord came vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: 24:20 I said to them, The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Say to the house of Israel, Thus says the Lord God: I will profane my sanctuary, the pride of your power, the delight of your eyes, and your heart’s desire; and your sons and your daughters whom you left behind shall fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Say to the house of Israel thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! profaning my sanctuary. The pride of your strength, The delight of your eyes And the object of the tender regard of your soul,- And your sons and your daughters whom ye have left behind, by the sword, shall fall.
text.KJV: 24:21 Speake vnto the house of Israel; Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will prophane my sanctuarie, the excellencie of your strength, the desire of your eyes, and that which your soule pitieth; and your sonnes and your daughters, whom yee haue left, shall fall by the sword.
text.NABRE: 24:21 Say to the house of Israel: Thus says the Lord God: I will now desecrate my sanctuary, the pride of your strength, the delight of your eyes, the concern of your soul. The sons and daughters you left behind shall fall by the sword.

text.NRSV: And you shall do as I have done; you shall not cover your upper lip or eat the bread of mourners.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye do, as I have done.— The beard, shall ye not cover, And the bread of other men:, shall ye not eat;
text.KJV: 24:22 And yee shall doe as I haue done: yee shall not couer your lips, nor eate the bread of men.
text.NABRE: 24:22 Then you shall do as I have done, not covering your beards nor eating the bread of mourning.

text.NRSV: Your turbans shall be on your heads and your sandals on your feet; you shall not mourn or weep, but you shall pine away in your iniquities and groan to one another.
text.Rotherham: And your chaplets shall be on your heads. And your sandals on your feet, Ye shall neither lament, nor weep,- Yet shall ye pine away in your iniquities, and shall groan one to another.
text.KJV: 24:23 And your tires shall be vpon your heads, and your shooes vpon your feet: yee shall not mourne nor weepe, but yee shall pine away for your iniquities, and mourne one towards an other.
text.NABRE: 24:23 Your turbans shall remain on your heads, your sandals on your feet. You shall not mourn or weep, but you shall waste away because of your sins and groan to one another.

text.NRSV: Thus Ezekiel shall be a sign to you; you shall do just as he has done. When this comes, then you shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: So shall Ezekiel become to you a wonder, According to all that he hath done, shall ye do: When it cometh, then shall ye know that I am The Lord, Yahweh.
text.KJV: 24:24 Thus Ezekiel is vnto you a signe: according to all that he hath done, shall yee doe: and when this commeth, yee shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 24:24 Ezekiel shall be a sign for you: everything he did, you shall do. When it happens, you shall know that I am the Lord God.

text.NRSV: And you, mortal, on the day when I take from them their stronghold, their joy and glory, the delight of their eyes and their heart’s affection, and also their sons and their daughters,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore Son of man, Shall it not be— In the day when I take away from them Their strength, The joy of their adorning,— The delight of their eyes And the desire of their soul, Their sons and their daughters
text.KJV: 24:25 Also thou sonne of man, shall it not be in the day when I take from them their strength, the ioy of their glorie, the desire of their eyes, and that whereupon they set their minds, their sonnes and their daughters;
text.NABRE: 24:25 End of Ezekiel’s Muteness. As for you, son of man, truly, on the very day I take away from them their strength, their glorious joy, the delight of their eyes, the desire of their soul, the pride of their hearts, their sons and daughters,

text.NRSV: on that day, one who has escaped will come to you to report to you the news.
text.Rotherham: That in that day, one that hath escaped shall come in unto thee,—to cause thee to hear it with thine own ears?
text.KJV: 24:26 That he that escapeth in that day, shall come vnto thee, to cause thee to heare it with thine eares?
text.NABRE: 24:26 on that day a survivor will come to you so that you may hear it with your own ears.

text.NRSV: On that day your mouth shall be opened to the one who has escaped, and you shall speak and no longer be silent. So you shall be a sign to them; and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That in that day, thy mouth shall be opened unto him that hath escaped, And thou shalt speak and not be dumb any longer? So shalt thou become to them a wonder, And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 24:27 In that day shall thy mouth be opened to him which is escaped, and thou shalt speake & be no more dumbe, and thou shalt be a signe vnto them, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 24:27 On that day, with the survivor, your mouth shall be opened; you shall speak and be mute no longer. You shall be a sign to them, and they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 25:1 The word of the Lord came againe vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: 25:1 III. Prophecies Against Foreign Nations Chapter 25 Against Ammon. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward the Ammonites and prophesy against them.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Set thy face against the sons of Ammon,— and prophesy against them.
text.KJV: 25:2 Sonne of man, set thy face against the Ammonites, and prophecie against them,
text.NABRE: 25:2 Son of man, turn toward the Ammonites and prophesy against them.

text.NRSV: Say to the Ammonites, Hear the word of the Lord God: Thus says the Lord God, Because you said, “Aha!” over my sanctuary when it was profaned, and over the land of Israel when it was made desolate, and over the house of Judah when it went into exile;
text.Rotherham: So then thou shalt say to the sons of Ammon, Hear ye the word of My Lord Yahweh: Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh: Because thou saidst Aha! Against my sanctuary when it was profaned. And against the soil of Israel when it was laid waste, And against the house of Judah when they went into exile.
text.KJV: 25:3 And say vnto the Ammonites; Heare the word of the Lord God, Thus saith the Lord God; Because thou saidst, Aha, against my sanctuarie, when it was prophaned, and against the land of Israel, when it was desolate, & against the house of Iudah, when they went into captiuitie;
text.NABRE: 25:3 Say to the Ammonites: Hear the word of the Lord! Thus says the Lord God: Because you jeered at my sanctuary when it was desecrated, at the land of Israel when it was destroyed, and at the house of Judah when they went into exile,

text.NRSV: therefore I am handed you over to the people of the east for a possession. They shall set their encampments among you and pitch their tents in your midst; they shall eat your fruit, and they shall drink your milk.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! delivering thee up to the Sons of the East for a possession And they shall set their encampments in thee, And place in thee their habitations, They, shall eat thy fruits, And they, shall drink thy milk;
text.KJV: 25:4 Behold therefore, I will deliuer thee to the men of the East for a possession, and they shall set their palaces in thee, and make their dwellings in thee: they shall eate thy fruit, and they shall drinke thy milke.
text.NABRE: 25:4 therefore I am giving you to people from the east as a possession. They shall set up their encampments among you and pitch their tents in your midst; they shall eat your produce and drink your milk.

text.NRSV: I will make Rabbah a pasture for camels and Ammon a fold for flocks. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will make of Rabbah a home for camels, And the sons of Ammon a couching-place for flocks, So shall ye know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 25:5 And I will make Rabbah a stable for camels, and the Ammonites a couching place for flocks: and ye shal know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 25:5 And I will turn Rabbah into a pasture for camels and all of Ammon into a grazing place for flocks. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: Because you have clapped your hands and stamped your feet and rejoiced with all the malice within you against the land of Israel,
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Because thou didst clap thy hands and stamp with thy foot,—and didst rejoice with all thy contempt in thy soul, against the soil of Israel.
text.KJV: 25:6 For thus saith the Lord God, Because thou hast clapped thine hands and stamped with the feete, and reioyced in heart with all thy despite against the land of Israel:
text.NABRE: 25:6 For thus says the Lord God: Because you rejoiced over the land of Israel with scorn in your heart, clapping your hands and stamping your feet,

text.NRSV: therefore I have stretched out my hand against you, and will hand you over as spoil to the nations; I will cut you off from the peoples and will make you perish out of the countries; I will destroy you. Then you shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore behold me! I have stretched out my hand over thee And will deliver thee for a prey to the nations, And will cut thee off from among the peoples, And will cause thee to perish from among the lands,— I will destroy thee, So shalt thou know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 25:7 Behold therefore, I will stretch out mine hand vpon thee, and will deliuer thee for a spoile to the heathen, and I will cut thee off from the people, and I wil cause thee to perish out of the countreys: I will destroy thee, and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 25:7 therefore, see, I am stretching out my hand against you and giving you up as plunder to the nations. I will cut you off from the peoples and wipe you out of the lands. I will destroy you, and you shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because Moab and Seir said, “See, the house of Judah is like all the other nations,”
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh: Because Moab and Seir say, Lo! like all the nations, is the house of Judah.
text.KJV: 25:8 ¶ Thus saith the Lord God, Because that Moab and Seir doe say, Behold, the house of Iudah is like vnto all the heathen:
text.NABRE: 25:8 Against Moab. Thus says the Lord God: Because Moab said, “See! the house of Judah is like all the other nations,”

text.NRSV: therefore I will lay open the flank of Moab from the towns on its frontier, Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim, the glory of the country.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! laying open the side of Moab out of the cities, Out of his cities on his frontiers, The beauty of the land of Beth-jeshimoth, Baal-meon, and Kiriathaim:
text.KJV: 25:9 Therefore beholde, I will open the side of Moab from the cities, from his cities which are on his frontiers, the glory of the countrey Beth-ieshimoth, Baal-meon and Kiriathaim,
text.NABRE: 25:9 therefore, I am exposing the whole flank of Moab with its cities, the jewels of its land: Beth-jesimoth, Baalmeon, and Kiriathaim.

text.NRSV: I will give it along with the Ammonites to the people of the east as a possession, so that it may not be remembered among the nations.
text.Rotherham: To the Sons of the East [when they come] against the sons of Ammon, and I will deliver it up for possession,-to the end the sons of Ammon may not be remembered among the nations:
text.KJV: 25:10 Unto the men of the East with the Ammonites, and will giue them in possession, that the Ammonites may not be remembred among the nations.
text.NABRE: 25:10 I will hand it over, along with the Ammonites, to the people from the east that it may not be remembered among the nations.

text.NRSV: Thus I will execute judgments upon Moab. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Also upon Moab, will I execute judgments,- So shall they know that I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 25:11 And I will execute iudgments vpon Moab, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 25:11 I will execute judgment upon Moab that they may know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because Edom acted revengefully against the house of Judah and has incurred great guilt by taking revenge on them,
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Because of what Edom hath done, in taking vengeance on the house of Judah, so that they have become guilty again and again and have taken vengeance upon them,
text.KJV: 25:12 ¶ Thus saith the Lord God, Because that Edom hath dealt against the house of Iudah by taking vengeance, and hath greatly offended, and reuenged himselfe vpon them:
text.NABRE: 25:12 Against Edom. Thus says the Lord God: Because Edom took vengeance on the house of Judah and incurred terrible guilt by taking vengeance on them,

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God, I will stretch out my hand against Edom, and cut off from it humans and animals, and I will make it waste; from Teman even to Dedan they shall fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, I will therefore stretch forth my hand over Edom, and will cut off therefrom man and beast, And will deliver it up as a desolation, from Teman, And they of Dedan, by the sword, shall fall,
text.KJV: 25:13 Therefore thus saith the Lord God, I will also stretch out mine hand vpon Edom, and will cut off man and beast from it, and I will make it desolate from Teman, and they of Dedan shall fall by the sword.
text.NABRE: 25:13 therefore thus says the Lord God: I will stretch out my hand against Edom and cut off from it human being and beast alike. I will turn it into ruins from Teman to Dedan; they shall fall by the sword.

text.NRSV: I will lay my vengeance upon Edom by the hand of my people Israel; and they shall act in Edom according to my anger and according to my fury; and they shall know my vengeance, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And I will put forth mine avenging against Edom, by the hand of my people Israel, And they shall deal with Edom, according to mine anger and according to mine indignation,- So shall they know mine avenging, Declareth My Lord. Yahweh,
text.KJV: 25:14 And I will lay my vengeance vpon Edom by the hand of my people Israel, and they shall doe in Edom according to mine anger, and according to my furie, and they shall know my vengeance, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 25:14 I will put my vengeance against Edom into the hands of my people Israel; they will deal with Edom in accord with my furious anger. Thus they shall know my vengeance!—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: Because with unending hostilities the Philistines acted in vengeance, and with malice of heart took revenge in destruction;
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord Yahweh, Because of what the Philistines have done by way of vengeance,- In that they have taken vengeance with contempt in the soul, to destroy, with the enmity of age-past times.
text.KJV: 25:15 ¶ Thus saith the Lord God, Because the Philistines haue dealt by reuenge, and haue taken vengeance with a despiteful heart, to destroy it for the old hatred:
text.NABRE: 25:15 Against the Philistines. Thus says the Lord God: Because the Philistines acted vengefully and exacted vengeance with intentional malice, destroying with undying hostility,

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord God, I will stretch out my hand against the Philistines, cut off the Cherethites, and destroy the rest of the seacoast.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! stretching forth my hand over the Philistines, And I will cut off the Cherethim,— And will cause to perish the remnant of the coast of the sea;
text.KJV: 25:16 Therefore thus saith the Lord God, Behold, I will stretch out mine hand vpon the Philistines, and I will cut off the Cherethims, and destroy the remnant of the Seacoast.
text.NABRE: 25:16 therefore thus says the Lord God: See! I am stretching out my hand against the Philistines, and I will cut off the Cherethites and wipe out the remnant on the seacoast.

text.NRSV: I will execute great vengeance on them with fiery rebukes. Then they shall know that I am the Lord, when I lay my vengeance on them.
text.Rotherham: And I will execute upon them great vengeance, with rebukes of indignation: shall they know that I am Yahweh, when I put forth mine avenging against them.
text.KJV: 25:17 And I wil execute great vengeance vpon them with furious rebukes, and they shall knowe that I am the Lord, when I shall lay my vengeance vpon them.
text.NABRE: 25:17 Thus I will execute great acts of vengeance on them, punishing them furiously. Then they shall know that I am the Lord, when I wreak my vengeance on them.

text.NRSV: In the eleventh year, on the first day of the month, the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the eleventh year, on the first of the month, that the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 26:1 And it came to passe in the eleuenth yeere, in the first day of the moneth, that the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying,
text.NABRE: 26:1 Chapter 26 Against the City of Tyre. On the first day of the eleventh month of the eleventh year, the word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, because Tyre said concerning Jerusalem, “Aha, the gate of the peoples is broken, it has swung open to me; I shall be replenished, now that she is laid waste,”
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Because Tyre hath said concerning Jerusalem. Aha! She is broken that was the doors of the peoples. She is turned unto me,— I shall be filled, She is laid waste,
text.KJV: 26:2 Sonne of man, because that Tyrus hath said against Ierusalem, Aha, she is broken that was the gates of the people, she is turned vnto me, I shalbe replenished now she is laid waste:
text.NABRE: 26:2 Son of man, because Tyre said of Jerusalem: “Aha! The gateway of the peoples is smashed! It has been turned over to me; I will be enriched by its ruin!”

text.NRSV: therefore, thus says the Lord God: See, I am against you, O Tyre! I will hurl many nations against you, as the sea hurls its waves.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith My Lord Yahweh, Behold me! against thee O Tyre,— So then I will bring up against thee many nations, As the sea bringeth up its rolling waves;
text.KJV: 26:3 Therefore thus saith the Lord God, Behold, I am against thee, O Tyrus, and will cause many nations to come vp against thee, as the sea causeth his waues to come vp.
text.NABRE: 26:3 therefore thus says the Lord God: See! I am coming against you, Tyre; I will churn up against you many nations, just as the sea churns up its waves.

text.NRSV: They shall destroy the walls of Tyre and break down her towers. I will scrape her soil from her and make her a bare rock.
text.Rotherham: And they shall destroy the walls of Tyre. And break down her towers, And I will scrape clean her dust from off her, —And make of her the glaring face of a cliff:
text.KJV: 26:4 And they shall destroy the walles of Tyrus, and breake downe her towres: I will also scrape her dust from her, and make her like the top of a rocke.
text.NABRE: 26:4 They will destroy the walls of Tyre and tear down its towers; I will scrape off its debris and leave it a bare rock.

text.NRSV: She shall become a place for spreading nets in the midst of the sea; for I have spoken, says the Lord God. She shall become spoil for the nations;
text.Rotherham: A place for the spreading of nets, shall she become, in the midst of the sea, For, I, have spoken Declareth My Lord. Yahweh,-And she shall become a prey to the nations.
text.KJV: 26:5 It shall be a place for the spreading of nets in the middest of the sea: for I haue spoken it, saith the Lord God, and it shall become a spoile to the nations.
text.NABRE: 26:5 It will become a place for drying nets in the midst of the sea. For I have spoken—oracle of the Lord God: she will become plunder for the nations.

text.NRSV: and her daughter-towns in the mainland shall be killed by the sword. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And her daughters that are in the field: with the sword shall be slain: So shall they know that I am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:6 And her daughters which are in the field shall be slaine by the sword, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 26:6 Her daughter cities on the mainland will be slaughtered by the sword; then they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: I will bring against Tyre from the north King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon, king of kings, with horses and chariots and cavalry and a great army.
text.Rotherham: For thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! bringing against Tyre. Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon out of the North. a king of kings,—with horses and with chariots and with horsemen, and a gathered host, and much people.
text.KJV: 26:7 ¶ For thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I will bring vpon Tyrus, Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, a king of kings, from the North, with horses, and with charets, and with horsemen, and companies, and much people.
text.NABRE: 26:7 Indeed thus says the Lord God: I am bringing up against Tyre from the north, Nebuchadnezzar, King of Babylon, king of kings, with horses and chariots, with cavalry, and a mighty horde of troops.

text.NRSV: Your daughter-towns in the mainland he shall put to the sword. He shall set up a siege-wall against you, cast up a mound against you, and raise a roof of shields against you.
text.Rotherham: Thy daughters in the field—with the sword, shall he slay,- And shall set against thee a siege-wall And cast up against thee an earthwork, And set up against thee a large shield;
text.KJV: 26:8 Hee shall slay with the sword thy daughters in the field, and he shal make a fort against thee, and cast a mount against thee, and lift vp the buckler against thee.
text.NABRE: 26:8 Your daughter cities on the mainland he shall slay with the sword. He shall build a siege wall around you, throw up a ramp against you, and raise his shields about you.

text.NRSV: He shall direct the shock of his battering rams against your walls and break down your towers with his axes.
text.Rotherham: And the stroke of his attacking-engine, will he direct against thy walls,— And thy towers, will he break down with his axes.
text.KJV: 26:9 Hee shall set engines of warre against thy walles, and with his axes he shall breake downe thy towres.
text.NABRE: 26:9 He shall pound your walls with battering-rams and break down your towers with his axes.

text.NRSV: His horses shall be so many that their dust shall cover you. At the noise of cavalry, wheels, and chariots your very walls shall shake, when he enters your gates as one enters a city that has been breached.
text.Rotherham: By reason of the multitude of his horses, their dust shall cover thee,— At the noise of horseman and wheel and chariot, shall thy walls tremble, when he entereth into thy gates Just as they do who enter a city broken open.
text.KJV: 26:10 By reason of the abundance of his horses, their dust shall couer thee: thy walles shall shake at the noise of the horsemen, and of the wheeles, and of the charets, when he shall enter into thy gates, as men enter into a citie wherein is made a breach.
text.NABRE: 26:10 From the surging of his horses he will cover you with dust; from the noise of warhorses, wheels and chariots. Your walls will shake when he enters your gates, even as one enters a city that is breached.

text.NRSV: With the hoofs of his horses he shall trample all your streets. He shall put your people to the sword, and your strong pillars shall fall to the ground.
text.Rotherham: With the hoofs of his horses, shall he tread down all thy streets,— Thy people-with the sword, shall he slay, And thy pillars of strength to the earth, shall go down.
text.KJV: 26:11 With the hoofes of his horses shall he tread downe all thy streets: he shall slay thy people by the sword, and thy strong garisons shall goe downe to the ground.
text.NABRE: 26:11 With the hooves of his horses he will trample all your streets; Your people he will slay by the sword; your mighty pillars will collapse.

text.NRSV: They shall plunder your riches and loot your merchandise; they shall break down your walls and destroy your fine houses. Your stones and timber and soil they shall cast into the water.
text.Rotherham: And they shall spoil thy wealth And make a prey of thy merchandise, And break down thy walls And thy pleasant houses, shall they lay in ruins; And thy stones and thy timber and thy dust—in the midst of the waters, shall they lay.
text.KJV: 26:12 And they shall make a spoile of thy riches, and make a pray of thy merchandise, and they shall breake downe thy walles, and destroy thy pleasant houses, and they shall lay thy stones, and thy timber, and thy dust in the midst of the water.
text.NABRE: 26:12 They shall plunder your wealth and pillage your goods; They will tear down your walls and demolish your splendid houses. Your stones, timbers, and debris they will cast into the sea.

text.NRSV: I will silence the music of your songs; the sound of your lyres shall be heard no more.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause to cease the hum of thy songs; And the sound of thy lyres, shall not be heard any more.
text.KJV: 26:13 And I wil cause the noise of thy songs to cease, and the sound of thy harpes shalbe no more heard.
text.NABRE: 26:13 I will bring an end to the noise of your songs; the music of your lyres will be heard no more.

text.NRSV: I will make you a bare rock; you shall be a place for spreading nets. You shall never again be rebuilt, for I the Lord have spoken, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And I will make of thee the glaring face of a cliff. A place for the spreading of nets, shalt thou become, Thou shalt not be but any more, For I. Yahweh have spoken it, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:14 And I will make thee like the top of a rocke: they shall bee a place to spread nets vpon: thou shalt bee built no more: for I the Lord haue spoken it, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 26:14 I will turn you into bare rock, you will become a place for drying nets. You shall never be rebuilt, for I the Lord have spoken— oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: Shall not the coastlands shake at the sound of your fall, when the wounded groan, when slaughter goes on within you?
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, to Tyre: Shall it not be that. At the sound of thy fall. With the groan of thy pierced one. With the slaughter made in thy midst, The Coastlands shall tremble?
text.KJV: 26:15 ¶ Thus saith the Lord God to Tyrus; Shall not the Iles shake at the sound of thy fall, when the wounded crie, when the slaughter is made in the midst of thee?
text.NABRE: 26:15 Thus says the Lord God to Tyre: At the sound of your downfall, at the groaning of the wounded, When victims are slain within you, will the islands not quake?

text.NRSV: Then all the princes of the sea shall step down from their thrones; they shall remove their robes and strip off their embroidered garments. They shall clothe themselves with trembling, and shall sit on the ground; they shall tremble every moment, and be appalled at you.
text.Rotherham: Then shall come down from off their thrones all the princes of the sea, And shall lay aside their robes. And their embroidered garments, shall strip off,— With tremblings, shall they clothe them. selves Upon the ground, shall they sit, And shall tremble every moment and be astonished over thee.
text.KJV: 26:16 Then all the Princes of the sea shall come downe from their thrones, and lay away their robes, and put off their broidred garments: they shall cloth themselues with trembling, they shall sit vpon the ground, and shall tremble at euery moment, and be astonished at thee.
text.NABRE: 26:16 All the princes of the sea will step down from their thrones, Lay aside their robes, and strip off their embroidered garments. Clothed in mourning, they will sit on the ground And tremble, horror-struck and appalled at you.

text.NRSV: And they shall raise a lamentation over you, and say to you: How you have vanished from the seas, O city of renown, once mighty on the sea, you and your inhabitants, who imposed your terror on all the mainland!
text.Rotherham: Then shall they take up over thee a dirge, And say to thee,- How hast thou perished, And ceased from the seas,- The city renowned which was strong in the sea She and they who dwelt in her, Who imparted their terror To all who dwelt in her.
text.KJV: 26:17 And they shall take vp a lamentation for thee, and say to thee, How art thou destroyed that wast inhabited of Sea-faring men, the renowned citie, which wast strong in the sea, she and her inhabitants, which cause their terrour to be on all that haunt it?
text.NABRE: 26:17 They will raise lament over you and say to you: How you have perished, gone from the seas, Renowned City! Once she was mighty on the sea, she and her inhabitants, Those who spread their terror to all who dwelt nearby.

text.NRSV: Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall; the islands in the sea are dismayed at your passing.
text.Rotherham: Now, shall tremble the Coastlands, In the day of thy fall,- And shall shudder the isles that are in the sea. At thine exit!
text.KJV: 26:18 Now shall the Iles tremble in the day of thy fall, yea the Iles that are in the sea, shall bee troubled at thy departure.
text.NABRE: 26:18 On this, the day of your fall, the islands quake! The islands in the sea are terrified at your passing.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord God: When I make you a desolate city, like the cities that are not inhabited, when I bring up the deep over you, and the great waters cover you,
text.Rotherham: For thus saith My Lord Yahweh, When I make thee a desolate city, Like cities which cannot be dwelt in,- When I bring up over thee the roaring deep, And the mighty waters cover thee,
text.KJV: 26:19 For thus saith the Lord God; When I shal make thee a desolate citie, like the cities that are not inhabited, when I shall bring vp the deepe vpon thee, and great waters shall couer thee;
text.NABRE: 26:19 Indeed thus says the Lord God: When I make you a ruined city like cities no longer inhabited, When I churn up the deep and its mighty waters cover you,

text.NRSV: then I will thrust you down with those who descend into the Pit, to the people of long ago, and I will make you live in the world below, among ancient ruins, with those who go down to the Pit, so that you will not be inhabited and have a place in the land of the living.
text.Rotherham: Then will I bring thee down with them that go down into the pit. Unto the people of age-past times And cause thee to dwell in the earth below Among the desolations from age-past times With them that go down into the pit That thou mayest not be dwelt in, Nor yet present thyself in the land of the living.
text.KJV: 26:20 When I shall bring thee downe, with them that descend into the pit, with the people of old time, and shall set thee in the low parts of the earth, in places desolate of olde, with them that goe downe to the pit, that thou bee not inhabited, and I shall set glorie in the land of the liuing:
text.NABRE: 26:20 Then I will thrust you down with those who go down to the pit, to those of the bygone age; I will make you dwell in the netherworld, in the everlasting ruins, with those who have gone down to the pit, So you will never return or have a place in the land of the living.

text.NRSV: I will bring you to a dreadful end, and you shall be no more; though sought for, you will never be found again, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: A terror, will I make thee And thou shalt not be: Though thou be sought for, Thou shalt not be found any more To times age-abiding, Declareth My Lord, Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:21 I will make thee a terrour, and thou shalt bee no more: though thou be sought for, yet shalt thou neuer bee found againe, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 26:21 I will make you a horror, and you shall be no more; You shall be sought for, but never found again— oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 27:1 The word of the Lord came againe vnto mee, saying,
text.NABRE: 27:1 Chapter 27 The Ship Tyre. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Now you, mortal, raise a lamentation over Tyre,
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, son of man, Take up over Tyre a dirge;
text.KJV: 27:2 Now thou sonne of man, take vp a lamentation for Tyrus;
text.NABRE: 27:2 You, son of man, raise a lament over Tyre,

text.NRSV: and say to Tyre, which sits at the entrance to the sea, merchant of the peoples on many coastlands, Thus says the Lord God: O Tyre, you have said, “I am perfect in beauty.”
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt say unto Tyre, O thou that dwellest by the entrances of the sea, Thou merchant of the peoples unto many shores,— Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh, O Tyre, Thou saidst, I, am the perfection of beauty!
text.KJV: 27:3 And say vnto Tyrus, O thou that art situate at the entrie of the sea, which art a merchant of the people for many Iles, Thus saith the Lord God; O Tyrus, thou hast said, I am of perfit beautie.
text.NABRE: 27:3 and say to Tyre, who sits at the entrance to the sea, trader to peoples on many coastlands, Thus says the Lord God: Tyre, you said, “I am a ship, perfect in beauty”;

text.NRSV: Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders made perfect your beauty.
text.Rotherham: In the heart of the seas, were thy bounds, —Thy builders perfected thy beauty:
text.KJV: 27:4 Thy borders are in the midst of the seas, thy builders haue perfected thy beautie.
text.NABRE: 27:4 In the heart of the sea was your territory; your builders perfected your beauty.

text.NRSV: They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; they took a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
text.Rotherham: With fir-trees from Senir, but they for thee all the two-decked vessels,— Cedar from Lebanon, fetched they, to make masts for thee:
text.KJV: 27:5 They haue made all thy shippe bords of firre trees of Senir: they haue taken Cedars from Lebanon, to make mastes for thee.
text.NABRE: 27:5 With juniper wood from Senir they built all your decks; A cedar from Lebanon they took to make you a mast.

text.NRSV: Of oaks of Bashan they made your oars; they made your deck of pines from the coasts of Cyprus, inlaid with ivory.
text.Rotherham: With oaks from Bashan, made they thine oars,— Thy benches, made they of ivory, inlaid with boxwood, From the shores of the West:
text.KJV: 27:6 Of the okes of Bashan haue they made thine oares: the companie of the Ashurites haue made thy benches of Yuorie, brought out of the Iles of Chittim.
text.NABRE: 27:6 With oaks of Bashan they fashioned your oars, Your bridge, of ivory-inlaid cypress wood from the coasts of Kittim.

text.NRSV: Of fine embroidered linen from Egypt was your sail, serving as your ensign; blue and purple from the coasts of Elishah was your awning.
text.Rotherham: Of fine linen with embroidered work from Egypt, was thy sail, To serve thee for ensign,— Blue and purple from the shores of Aeolis became thine awning:
text.KJV: 27:7 Fine linnen with broidred worke from Egypt, was that which thou spreddest forth to be thy saile, blew and purple from the Iles of Elishah was that which couered thee.
text.NABRE: 27:7 Fine embroidered linen from Egypt became your sail; Your awnings were made of purple and scarlet from the coasts of Elishah.

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; skilled men of Zemer were within you, they were your pilots.
text.Rotherham: the inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad, Became rowers for thee,— Thine own skilled men O Tyre, were within thee, They were thy sailors:
text.KJV: 27:8 The inhabitants of Zidon and Aruad were thy mariners: thy wise men, O Tyrus, that were in thee, were thy pilots.
text.NABRE: 27:8 Inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your oarsmen; Your own sages, Tyre, were on board, serving as your sailors.

text.NRSV: The elders of Gebal and its skilled men were within you, caulking your seams; all the ships of the sea with their mariners were within you, to barter for your wares.
text.Rotherham: the elders of Byblus and the skilled men thereof, were within thee, Thy calkers,— All the ships of the sea and their mariners, were within thee, To carry on thy traffic,
text.KJV: 27:9 The ancients of Gebal, and the wise men thereof were in thee thy calkers, all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in thee, to occupie thy merchandise.
text.NABRE: 27:9 The elders and sages of Gebal were with you to caulk your seams. Every ship and sailor on the sea came to you to carry on trade.

text.NRSV: Persia and Lud and Put were in your army as your warriors; they hung shield and helmet in you; they gave you splendor.
text.Rotherham: They of Persia and Lud and Phut were in thine army, Thy men of war,— Shield and helmet, hung they up within thee, They set forth thy splendour:
text.KJV: 27:10 They of Persia, and of Lud, and of Phut were in thine army, thy men of warre: they hanged the shield and helmet in thee: they set forth thy comelines.
text.NABRE: 27:10 Persia and Lud and Put were warriors in your army; Shield and helmet they hung on you to enhance your splendor.

text.NRSV: Men of Arvad and Helech were on your walls all around; men of Gamad were in your towers. They hung their quivers all around your walls; they made perfect your beauty.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Arvad, :with thine army, were upon thy walls round about, And valorous in thy towers,— Their shields, hung they up on thy walls round about, They perfected thy beauty:
text.KJV: 27:11 The men of Aruad with thine armie were vpon thy wals round about, and the Gammadims were in thy towres: they hanged their shields vpon thy wals round about: they haue made thy beautie perfect.
text.NABRE: 27:11 The men of Arvad and Helech were on your walls all around And Gamadites on your towers; they hung their shields around your walls, they made your beauty perfect.

text.NRSV: Tarshish did business with you because of your great wealth of every kind; silver, iron, tin, and lead they exchanged for your wares.
text.Rotherham: Tarshish was a trader of thine. For the abundance of all wealth,— With silver iron. tin and lead, Took they part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: 27:12 Tarshish was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of all kinde of riches: with siluer, yron, tinne, and lead they traded in thy faires.
text.NABRE: 27:12 Tarshish traded with you, so great was your wealth, Exchanging for your wares silver, iron, tin, and lead.

text.NRSV: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech traded with you; they exchanged human beings and vessels of bronze for your merchandise.
text.Rotherham: Javan. Tubal and Meshech, They were merchants of thine,— With the persons of men and vessels of bronze, Shared they in thy barter:
text.KJV: 27:13 Iauan, Tubal and Meshech, they were thy merchants: they traded the persons of men, and vessels of brasse in thy market.
text.NABRE: 27:13 Javan, Tubal, and Meshech also traded with you, Exchanging slaves and bronze vessels for your merchandise.

text.NRSV: Beth-togarmah exchanged for your wares horses, war horses, and mules.
text.Rotherham: Some of the house of Togarmah, with horses and horsemen and mules, Took part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: 27:14 They of the house of Togarmah traded in thy faires with horses, and horsemen, and mules.
text.NABRE: 27:14 Horses, steeds, and mules from Beth-togarmah were exchanged for your wares.

text.NRSV: The Rhodians traded with you; many coastlands were your stockholders; ivory tusks and ebony they brought you in payment.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Dedan were merchants of thine, Many, isles, took the merchandise of thy hand, —Horns of ivory and ebony, gave they back to thee in exchange:
text.KJV: 27:15 The men of Dedan were thy merchants, many Iles were the merchandise of thine hand: they brought thee for a present, hornes of Iuorie, and Ebenie.
text.NABRE: 27:15 Men of Rhodes trafficked with you; many coastlands were your agents; Ivory tusks and ebony wood they brought back as your payment.

text.NRSV: Edom did business with you because of your many goods; they exchanged for your wares turquoise, purple, embroidered work, fine linen, coral, and rubies.
text.Rotherham: Syria, was a trader of thine For the abundance of thy manufactures,- With carbuncle and purple and embroidery and fine linen and coral and rubies, Took they part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: 27:16 Syria was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of the wares of thy making: they occupied in thy faires with Emeraulds, purple, and broidered worke, and fine linen, and Corall, and Agate.
text.NABRE: 27:16 Edom traded with you for your many wares: garnets, purple dye, embroidered cloth, Fine linen, coral, and rubies they gave you as merchandise.

text.NRSV: Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged for your merchandise wheat from Minnith, millet, honey, oil, and balm.
text.Rotherham: Judah and the land of Israel, They, were merchants of thine,— With the wheat of Minnith and the sweets of pannag and honey and oil and balsam, Shared they in thy barter:
text.KJV: 27:17 Iudah and the land of Israel they were thy merchants: they traded in thy market wheate of Minnith, and Pannag, and honie, and oyle, & balme.
text.NABRE: 27:17 Judah and the land of Israel trafficked with you: Minnith wheat, grain, honey, oil, and balm they gave you as merchandise.

text.NRSV: Damascus signaled business with you because of your many goods—because of your great wealth of every kind—wine of Helbon, and white wool.
text.Rotherham: Damascus was a trader of thine For the multitude of thy manufactures For the multitude of all wealth,- With the wine of Helbon and white wool:
text.KJV: 27:18 Damascus was thy merchant in the multitude of the wares of thy making, for the multitude of all riches: in the wine of Helbon, and white wooll.
text.NABRE: 27:18 Damascus traded with you for your many wares, so great was your wealth, exchanging Helbon wine and Zahar wool.

text.NRSV: Vedan and Javan from Uzal entered into trade for your wares; wrought iron, cassia, and sweet cane were bartered for your merchandise.
text.Rotherham: Wedan and Javan, from Uzal, Brought into thy traffic,— Steel, cassia and calamus, Were, in thy merchandise:
text.KJV: 27:19 Dan also and Iauan going to and fro, occupyed in thy faires: bright yron, Cassia and Calamus were in thy market.
text.NABRE: 27:19 Javan exchanged wrought iron, cassia, and aromatic cane from Uzal for your wares.

text.NRSV: Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
text.Rotherham: Dedan was a merchant of thine,- With spreading wraps for riding:
text.KJV: 27:20 Dedan was thy merchant in precious clothes for charets.
text.NABRE: 27:20 Dedan traded with you for riding gear.

text.NRSV: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your favored dealers in lambs, rams, and goats; in these they did business with you.
text.Rotherham: Arabia and all the princes of Kedar, They, were traders at thy service,— With lambs and rams and he-goats, With these, were they traders of thine:
text.KJV: 27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar, they occupied with thee in lambes and rammes and goats: in these were they thy merchants.
text.NABRE: 27:21 Arabia and the sheikhs of Kedar were your agents, dealing in lambs, rams, and goats.

text.NRSV: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; they exchanged for your wares the best of all kinds of spices, and all precious stones, and gold.
text.Rotherham: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah, They were merchants of thine,— With the chief of all spices and with every kind of precious stone and gold, Took they part in thy traffic:
text.KJV: 27:22 The merchants of Shebah and Raamah, they were thy merchants: they occupied in thy faires with chiefe of all spices, and with all precious stones and golde.
text.NABRE: 27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah also traded with you, exchanging for your wares the very best spices, all kinds of precious stones, and gold.

text.NRSV: Haran, Canneh, Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad traded with you.
text.Rotherham: Haran and Canneh and Eden, the merchants of Sheba,—Asshur. Chilmad, Were merchants of thine:
text.KJV: 27:23 Haran, and Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Shebah, Asshur and Chilmad were thy merchants.
text.NABRE: 27:23 Haran, Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad,

text.NRSV: These traded with you in choice garments, in clothes of blue and embroidered work, and in carpets of colored material, bound with cords and made secure; in these they traded with you.
text.Rotherham: Those were thy merchants for gorgeous garments, for wrappings of blue and embroidery, and for treasure-chests of variegated cloth,—for cords, twisted and strong, in thy market:
text.KJV: 27:24 These were thy merchants in all sorts of things, in blewe clothes and broidered worke, and in chests of rich apparell, bound with cordes and made of Cedar among thy merchandise.
text.NABRE: 27:24 Traded with you, marketing rich garments, purple cloth, embroidered fabric, varicolored carpets, and braided cords.

text.NRSV: The ships of Tarshish carried your merchandise. So you were filled and made very glorious in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: the ships of Tarshish, were thy masts Carrying on thy barter, So wast thou filled and becamest very glorious In the heart of the seas.
text.KJV: 27:25 The ships of Tarshish did sing of thee in thy market, and thou wast replenished, and made very glorious in the middest of the seas.
text.NABRE: 27:25 The ships of Tarshish sailed for you with your goods; You were full and heavily laden in the heart of the sea.

text.NRSV: Your rowers have brought you into the high seas. The east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: Into mighty waters, have they brought thee, the rowers—even thee,— the east wind, hath broken thee In the heart of the seas:
text.KJV: 27:26 ¶ Thy rowers haue brought thee into great waters: the East winde hath broken thee in the middest of the Seas.
text.NABRE: 27:26 Out into deep waters your oarsmen brought you; The east wind shattered you in the heart of the sea.

text.NRSV: Your riches, your wares, your merchandise, your mariners, and your pilots, your caulkers, your dealers in merchandise, and all your warriors within you, with all the company that is with you, sink into the heart of the seas on the day of your ruin.
text.Rotherham: Thy wealth and thy wares, thy merchandise thy mariners, and thy sailors,-thy calkers and the barterers of thy merchandise and all thy men of war who are in thee and in all thy gathered host which is in thy midst Shall fall into the heart of the seas, In the day of thy fall.
text.KJV: 27:27 Thy riches and thy faires, thy merchandise, thy mariners, and thy pilots, thy calkers, and the occupiers of thy merchandise, and all thy men of warre that are in thee, and in all thy company, which is in the midst of thee, shall fall into the middest of the seas, in the day of thy ruine.
text.NABRE: 27:27 Your wealth, your goods, your wares, your sailors, your crew, The caulkers of your seams, those who traded for your goods, All the warriors with you, the whole crowd with you Sank into the heart of the sea on the day of your downfall.

text.NRSV: At the sound of the cry of your pilots the countryside shakes,
text.Rotherham: At the sound of the outcry of thy pilots, the coasts shall quake:
text.KJV: 27:28 The suburbs shall shake at the sound of the crie of thy pilots.
text.NABRE: 27:28 At the sound of your sailors’ shouts the waves shudder,

text.NRSV: and down from their ships come all that handle the oar. The mariners and all the pilots of the sea stand on the shore
text.Rotherham: Then shall come down from their ships all that handle the oar, mariners, all the sailors of the sea— Upon the land, shall they take up their station;
text.KJV: 27:29 And all that handle the oare; the mariners, and all the pilots of the Sea, shal come downe from their ships, they shall stand vpon the land;
text.NABRE: 27:29 Down from their ships come all who ply the oars; Sailors, all the seafaring crew, stand on the shore.

text.NRSV: and wail aloud over you, and cry bitterly. They throw dust on their heads and wallow in ashes;
text.Rotherham: And make loud [lament] over thee, with their voice, And make biter outcry,- And shall lift up dust on their heads, In ashes, shall roll themselves;
text.KJV: 27:30 And shall cause their voice to be heard against thee, and shall crie bitterly, and shall cast vp dust vpon their heads, they shall wallow themselues in the ashes.
text.NABRE: 27:30 They raise their voices over you and shout their bitter cries; They pour dust on their heads and cover themselves with ashes.

text.NRSV: they make themselves bald for you, and put on sackcloth, and they weep over you in bitterness of soul, with bitter mourning.
text.Rotherham: And shall make bald for thee a baldness And gird them with sackcloth,— And shall weep for thee in bitterness of soul A bitter lamentation;
text.KJV: 27:31 And they shall make themselues vtterly balde for thee, and girde them with sackecloth, and they shall weepe for thee with bitternesse of heart and bitter wailing.
text.NABRE: 27:31 For you they shave their heads bald and put on sackcloth; For you they weep bitterly, in anguished lament.

text.NRSV: In their wailing they raise a lamentation for you, and lament over you: “Who was ever destroyed like Tyre in the midst of the sea?”
text.Rotherham: And shall take up for thee in their Wailing: a dirge, And shall chant over thee,- Who is like Tyre? Like the Sent One in the midst of the sea?
text.KJV: 27:32 And in their wailing, they shall take vp a lamentation for thee, and lament ouer thee, saying; What citie is like Tyrus, like the destroyed in the middest of the sea?
text.NABRE: 27:32 They raise a lament for you; they wail over you: “Who was ever destroyed like Tyre in the midst of the sea?”

text.NRSV: When your wares came from the seas, you satisfied many peoples; with your abundant wealth and merchandise you enriched the kings of the earth.
text.Rotherham: By the going forth of thy commodities out of the seas, Thou didst satisfy many peoples, By the multitude of thy wares and of thy merchandise, Thou didst enrich the kings of the earth.
text.KJV: 27:33 When thy wares went foorth out of the seas, thou filledst many people, thou didst enrich the kings of the earth, with the multitude of thy riches, and of thy merchandise.
text.NABRE: 27:33 By exporting your goods by sea you satisfied many peoples, With your great wealth and merchandise you enriched the kings of the earth.

text.NRSV: Now you are wrecked by the seas, in the depths of the waters; your merchandise and all your company have sunk with you.
text.Rotherham: Now, thou art wrecked out of the seas In the depths of waters,— Thy merchandise and all thy gathered host In thy midst, have fallen.
text.KJV: 27:34 In the time when thou shalt be broken by the seas in the depths of the waters, thy merchandise and all thy companie in the middest of thee shal fall.
text.NABRE: 27:34 Now you are wrecked in the sea, in the watery depths; Your wares and all your crew have fallen down with you.

text.NRSV: All the inhabitants of the coastlands are appalled at you; and their kings are horribly afraid, their faces are convulsed.
text.Rotherham: All the dwellers in the coastlands, Are astounded over thee,- And their kings have been made to shudder a shuddering, To be troubled in countenance.
text.KJV: 27:35 All the inhabitants of the Iles shall bee astonished at thee, and their kings shall be sore afraid, they shall be troubled in their countenance.
text.NABRE: 27:35 All who dwell on the coastlands are aghast over you; Their kings are terrified, their faces distorted.

text.NRSV: The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.
text.Rotherham: The traders among the peoples, have hissed over thee: A terror, hast thou become, And, art not, Unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 27:36 The merchants among the people shall hisse at thee, thou shalt bee a terrour, and neuer shalt be any more.
text.NABRE: 27:36 The traders among the peoples now hiss at you; You have become a horror, you shall be no more.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying
text.KJV: 28:1 The word of the Lord came againe vnto me, saying,
text.NABRE: 28:1 Chapter 28 The Prince of Tyre. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, say to the prince of Tyre, Thus says the Lord God: Because your heart is proud and you have said, “I am a god; I sit in the seat of the gods, in the heart of the seas,” yet you are but a mortal, and no god, though you compare your mind with the mind of a god.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Say to the Prince of Tyre. Thus, saith My Lord. Yahweh— Because lofty, is thy heart and thou hast said A GOD, am I, In the seat of God, have I taken my seat In the heart of the seas; Whereas thou art a man, and not a GOD, But hast set thy heart as the heart of God:
text.KJV: 28:2 Sonne of man, say vnto the prince of Tyrus, Thus saith the Lord God; Because thine heart is lifted vp, and thou hast said, I am a God, I sit in the seate of God in the middest of the seas; yet thou art a man and not God, though thou set thine heart as the heart of God.
text.NABRE: 28:2 Son of man, say to the prince of Tyre: Thus says the Lord God: Because you are haughty of heart, you say, “I am a god! I sit on a god’s throne in the heart of the sea!” But you are a man, not a god; yet you pretend you are a god at heart!

text.NRSV: You are indeed wiser than Daniel; no secret is hidden from you;
text.Rotherham: Lo! wiser, thou than Daniel,— No secret, have they hidden from thee:
text.KJV: 28:3 Behold, thou art wiser then Daniel: there is no secret that they can hide from thee.
text.NABRE: 28:3 Oh yes, you are wiser than Daniel, nothing secret is too obscure for you!

text.NRSV: by your wisdom and your understanding you have amassed wealth for yourself, and have gathered gold and silver into your treasuries.
text.Rotherham: In thy wisdom and in thine understanding, hast thou gotten thee wealth,— And hast gotten gold and silver into thy treasuries:
text.KJV: 28:4 With thy wisedome and with thine vnderstanding thou hast gotten thee riches, and hast gotten gold and siluer into thy treasures.
text.NABRE: 28:4 By your wisdom and intelligence you made yourself rich, filling your treasuries with gold and silver.

text.NRSV: By your great skill in trade you have increased your wealth, and your heart has become proud in your wealth.
text.Rotherham: By the greatness of thy wisdom and by thy traffic, hast thou multiplied thy riches, -And thy heart hath become lofty in thy riches:
text.KJV: 28:5 By thy great wisedome, and by thy traffique hast thou increased thy riches, and thine heart is lifted vp because of thy riches.
text.NABRE: 28:5 Through your great wisdom in trading you heaped up riches for yourself— your heart is haughty because of your riches.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you compare your mind with the mind of a god,
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus saith My Lord Yahweh,— Because thou hast set thy heart as the heart of God,
text.KJV: 28:6 Therefore thus saith the Lord God; Because thou hast set thine heart as the heart of God;
text.NABRE: 28:6 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you pretend you are a god at heart,

text.NRSV: therefore, I will bring strangers against you, the most terrible of the nations; they shall draw their swords against the beauty of your wisdom and defile your splendor.
text.Rotherham: Therefore behold me! bringing upon thee aliens, The terrible ones of the nations,- And they shall unsheathe their swords over the beauty of thy wisdom, And profane thy splendour:
text.KJV: 28:7 Behold therefore, I will bring strangers vpon thee, the terrible of the nations: and they shall draw their swords against the beautie of thy wisedome, & they shall defile thy brightnesse.
text.NABRE: 28:7 Therefore, I will bring against you strangers, the most bloodthirsty of nations. They shall draw their swords against your splendid wisdom, and violate your radiance.

text.NRSV: They shall thrust you down into the Pit, and you shall die a violent death in the heart of the seas.
text.Rotherham: To the pit, shall they take thee down,- And thou shalt die the deaths of one thrust through, In the heart of the seas.
text.KJV: 28:8 They shall bring thee downe to the pit, and thou shalt die the deaths of them, that are slaine in the middest of the seas.
text.NABRE: 28:8 They shall thrust you down into the pit: you shall die a violent death in the heart of the sea.

text.NRSV: Will you still say, “I am a god,” in the presence of those who kill you, though you are but a mortal, and no god, in the hands of those who wound you?
text.Rotherham: Wilt thou really say, God, am I, before him who is slaying thee; When, thou, art a man and not a God, in the hand of them who are piercing thee?
text.KJV: 28:9 Wilt thou yet say before him that slayeth thee, I am God? but thou shalt be a man, and no God in the hand of him that slayeth thee.
text.NABRE: 28:9 Then, face to face with your killers, will you still say, “I am a god”? No, you are a man, not a god, handed over to those who slay you.

text.NRSV: You shall die the death of the uncircumcised by the hand of strangers; for I have spoken, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: The deaths of the uncircumcised, shalt thou die by the hand of aliens,— For I have spoken, Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 28:10 Thou shalt die the deaths of the vncircumcised, by the hand of strangers: for I haue spoken it, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 28:10 You shall die the death of the uncircumcised handed over to strangers, for I have spoken—oracle of the Lord God.

text.NRSV: Moreover the word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: 28:11 ¶ Moreouer the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: 28:11 The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, raise a lamentation over the king of Tyre, and say to him, Thus says the Lord God: You were the signet of perfection, full of wisdom and perfect in beauty.
text.Rotherham: Son of man Take thou up a dirge over the king of Tyre,— And thou shalt say to him Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Thou wast of finished proportions, Full of wisdom and Perfect in beauty:
text.KJV: 28:12 Sonne of man, take vp a lamentation vpon the king of Tyrus, and say vnto him, Thus saith the Lord God; Thou sealest vp the summe, full of wisedome and perfect in beautie.
text.NABRE: 28:12 Son of man, raise a lament over the king of Tyre, and say to him: Thus says the Lord God: You were a seal of perfection, full of wisdom, perfect in beauty.

text.NRSV: You were in Eden, the garden of God; every precious stone was your covering, carnelian, chrysolite, and moonstone, beryl, onyx, and jasper, sapphire, turquoise, and emerald; and worked in gold were your settings and your engravings. On the day that you were created they were prepared.
text.Rotherham: In Eden the garden of God, thou wast. Of every precious stone, was thy covering-Sardius. topaz, and diamond, Chrysolite beryl, and jasper, Sapphire carbuncle and emerald,- And of gold, was the work of thy timbrels and thy flutes within thee, In the day thou wast created, were they prepared:
text.KJV: 28:13 Thou hast beene in Eden the garden of God; euery precious stone was thy couering, the Sardius, Topaze, and the Diamond, the Beril, the Onyx, and the Iasper, the Saphir, the Emeraude, and the Carbuncle and gold: the workmanship of thy tabrets and of thy pipes was prepared in thee, in the day that thou wast created.
text.NABRE: 28:13 In Eden, the garden of God, you lived; precious stones of every kind were your covering: Carnelian, topaz, and beryl, chrysolite, onyx, and jasper, sapphire, garnet, and emerald. Their mounts and settings were wrought in gold, fashioned for you the day you were created.

text.NRSV: With an anointed cherub as guardian I placed you; you were on the holy mountain of God; you walked among the stones of fire.
text.Rotherham: Thou, wast the anointed cherub that covered,— When I appointed thee, in the holy mount of God, thou wast, Amid stones of fire, thou didst walk to and fro:
text.KJV: 28:14 Thou art the annointed Cherub that couereth: and I haue set thee so; thou wast vpon the holy mountaine of God; thou hast walked vp and downe in the middest of the stones of fire.
text.NABRE: 28:14 With a cherub I placed you; I put you on the holy mountain of God, where you walked among fiery stones.

text.NRSV: You were blameless in your ways from the day that you were created, until iniquity was found in you.
text.Rotherham: Complete, wast thou in thy ways, from the day thou wast created,- Until perversity was found in thee.
text.KJV: 28:15 Thou wast perfect in thy wayes from the day that thou wast created, till iniquitie was found in thee.
text.NABRE: 28:15 Blameless were you in your ways from the day you were created, Until evil was found in you.

text.NRSV: In the abundance of your trade you were filled with violence, and you sinned; so I cast you as a profane thing from the mountain of God, and the guardian cherub drove you out from among the stones of fire.
text.Rotherham: By the abundance of thy traffic, they filled thy midst with violence And thou didst sin,- So I cast thee as profane out of the mountain of God. And destroyed thee O covering cherub, from amid the stones of fire:
text.KJV: 28:16 By the multitude of thy merchandise they haue filled the middest of thee with violence, and thou hast sinned: therefore I will cast thee as prophane out of the mountaine of God: and I wil destroy thee, O couering Cherub, from the middest of the stones of fire.
text.NABRE: 28:16 Your commerce was full of lawlessness, and you sinned. Therefore I banished you from the mountain of God; the cherub drove you out from among the fiery stones.

text.NRSV: Your heart was proud because of your beauty; you corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor. I cast you to the ground; I exposed you before kings, to feast their eyes on you.
text.Rotherham: Lofty, was thy heart in thy beauty, Thou didst corrupt thy wisdom, because of thy splendour,— Upon the earth, did I cast thee. Before kings, did I set thee That they might look at thee:
text.KJV: 28:17 Thine heart was lifted vp because of thy beautie, thou hast corrupted thy wisedome by reason of thy brightnesse: I will cast thee to the ground: I will lay thee before kings, that they may behold thee.
text.NABRE: 28:17 Your heart had grown haughty because of your beauty; You corrupted your wisdom because of your splendor. I cast you to the ground, I made you a spectacle in the sight of kings.

text.NRSV: By the multitude of your iniquities, in the unrighteousness of your trade, you profaned your sanctuaries. So I brought out fire from within you; it consumed you, and I turned you to ashes on the ground in the sight of all who saw you.
text.Rotherham: Owing to the abounding of thine iniquities. In the perversity of thy traffic, Thou didst profane thy sanctuaries, Therefore brought I forth fire out of thy midst. the same devoured thee, And I turned thee to ashes on the ground, Before the eyes of all beholding thee:
text.KJV: 28:18 Thou hast defiled thy sanctuaries by the multitude of thine iniquities, by the iniquitie of thy traffique: therefore will I bring forth a fire from the middest of thee, it shall deuoure thee: and I will bring thee to ashes vpon the earth in the sight of all them that behold thee.
text.NABRE: 28:18 Because of the enormity of your guilt, and the perversity of your trade, you defiled your sanctuary. I brought fire out of you; it devoured you; I made you ashes on the ground in the eyes of all who see you.

text.NRSV: All who know you among the peoples are appalled at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.
text.Rotherham: All that had known thee among the peoples were astounded over thee,— A terror, hast thou become, And, art not, Unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 28:19 All they that know thee among the people, shall be astonished at thee: thou shalt be a terrour, and neuer shalt thou be any more.
text.NABRE: 28:19 All the nations who knew you are appalled on account of you; You have become a horror, never to be again.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me saying:
text.KJV: 28:20 ¶ Againe the word of the Lord came vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: 28:20 Against Sidon. The word of the Lord came to me:

text.NRSV: Mortal, set your face toward Sidon, and prophesy against it,
text.Rotherham: Son of man, Set thy face towards Zidon,— And prophesy over it;
text.KJV: 28:21 Sonne of man, set thy face against Zidon, and prophecie against it,
text.NABRE: 28:21 Son of man, turn your face toward Sidon and prophesy against it.

text.NRSV: and say, Thus says the Lord God: See, I am against you, O Sidon, and I will gain glory in your midst. They shall know that I am the Lord when I execute judgments in it, and manifest my holiness in it;
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say— Thus saith My Lord. Yahweh, Behold me! against thee, O Zidon, Therefore will I get myself glory in thy midst,— [And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh,] When I have executed against her judgments And hallowed myself in her.
text.KJV: 28:22 And say, Thus saith the Lord God; Behold, I am against thee, O Zidon, and I will be glorified in the middest of thee: and they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall haue executed iudgements in her, and shall be sanctified in her.
text.NABRE: 28:22 Thus says the Lord God: Watch out! I am against you, Sidon; I will win glory for myself in your midst. They shall know that I am the Lord, when I deliver judgment upon it and manifest my holiness in it.

text.NRSV: for I will send pestilence into it, and bloodshed into its streets; and the dead shall fall in its midst, by the sword that comes against it on every side. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then I will send against her, pestilence and blood in her streets, And the wounded shall fall in her midst by the sword upon her from every side,— And they shall know that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 28:23 For I will send into her, pestilence, and blood into her streetes, and the wounded shall be iudged in the middest of her by the sword vpon her on euery side, and they shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 28:23 I will send disease into it; blood will fill its streets, Within it shall fall those slain by the sword raised against it on every side. Then they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: The house of Israel shall no longer find a pricking brier or a piercing thorn among all their neighbors who have treated them with contempt. And they shall know that I am the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: And the house of Israel shall no more have a stinging prickle or a wounding thorn, of any round about them who used to despise them,- So shall they know that, I, am The Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: 28:24 ¶ And there shall be no more a pricking briar vnto the house of Israel, nor any grieuing thorne of all that are round about them that despised them, and they shal know that I am the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 28:24 No longer will there be a thorn that tears or a brier that scratches for the house of Israel From the surrounding neighbors who despise them; thus they shall know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered, and manifest my holiness in them in the sight of the nations, then they shall settle on their own soil that I gave to my servant Jacob.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith My Lord, Yahweh, When I gather together the house of Israel, from the midst of the peoples among whom they have been scattered, and shall hallow myself in them before the eyes of the nations Then shall they dwell upon their own soil, which I gave to my servant—to Jacob:
text.KJV: 28:25 Thus saith the Lord God; When I shall haue gathered the house of Israel frō the people among whom they are scattered, and shall be sanctified in them in the sight of the heathen, then shall they dwell in their land, that I haue giuen to my seruant Iacob.
text.NABRE: 28:25 Thus says the Lord God: When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered, and I manifest my holiness through them in the sight of the nations, then they shall live on the land I gave my servant Jacob.

text.NRSV: They shall live in safety in it, and shall build houses and plant vineyards. They shall live in safety, when I execute judgments upon all their neighbors who have treated them with contempt. And they shall know that I am the Lord their God.
text.Rotherham: Yea they shall dwell thereupon securely, Yea shall build houses, and plant vineyards, and dwell securely,— When I have executed judgments on all that despised them on every side of them, So shall they know that, I, Yahweh am their God
